target.title: fix(sessions): strip delivery-mirror and gateway-injected messages from sessions_history (#85669)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #85669

The `sessions_history` tool returned raw transcript messages without filtering OpenClaw-internal `delivery-mirror` and `gateway-injected` assistant entries. These entries duplicate every real assistant turn in the session transcript, causing double messages in the dashboard session viewer and confusing tool callers.

Telegram delivery is unaffected — messages still send once.

## Root Cause

The `isTranscriptOnlyOpenClawAssistant` guard already exists in `pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts` and is applied in replay/context-building paths, but was **not applied** in the `sessions_history` tool pipeline at `sessions-history-tool.ts`. The tool fetched raw messages from `chat.history`, sanitized them, but returned `delivery-mirror`/`gateway-injected` entries unfiltered.

The docs at `docs/reference/transcript-hygiene.md` state that replay filters delivery-mirror and gateway-injected assistant turns — the tool should match this contract.

## Fix

**`src/agents/tools/chat-history-text.ts`**
- Add `TRANSCRIPT_ONLY_OPENCLAW_MODELS` set (`delivery-mirror`, `gateway-injected`)
- Export `stripTranscriptOnlyMessages()`: filters assistant messages with `provider=openclaw` and a transcript-only model value

**`src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.ts`**
- Import `stripTranscriptOnlyMessages`
- Apply it to `rawMessages` before the `includeTools` branch — delivery-mirror entries are excluded **regardless** of the `includeTools` flag (these are internal infrastructure, not tool messages)

**`src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts`**
- New test: `sessions_history filters delivery-mirror and gateway-injected messages unconditionally`
- Covers both `includeTools=false` (default) and `includeTools=true` paths
- Verifies real assistant messages are preserved, delivery-mirror/gateway-injected are absent

## Testing

```
pnpm tsc --noEmit  # passes
```

New test exercises both code paths. The pre-existing `_historyCallCount` lint warning at line 698 is unrelated to this PR (pre-existing in the file).

## Related

- Issue: #85669
- `isTranscriptOnlyOpenClawAssistant` reference: `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts:55`
- Existing filter pattern in `chat-history-text.ts`: `stripToolMessages()`

---

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** The `sessions_history` tool returned raw transcript messages without filtering OpenClaw-internal `delivery-mirror` and `gateway-injected` assistant entries. These entries duplicate every real assistant turn, causing double messages in the dashboard session viewer and confusing tool callers (other agents reading session history via the tool). Closes #85669. The existing `isTranscriptOnlyOpenClawAssistant` guard was already applied in replay/context-building paths (per `docs/reference/transcript-hygiene.md`) but was not applied to the `sessions_history` tool pipeline; this PR brings the tool into compliance with the documented contract by exporting a `stripTranscriptOnlyMessages()` helper and applying it in `sessions-history-tool.ts` before the `includeTools` branch — so transcript-only entries are excluded **regardless** of the `includeTools` flag (since they are internal infrastructure, not user-visible tool messages).

**Real environment tested:**
- Date: 2026-05-27T21:15:04Z
- Host: Darwin 25.4.0 arm64 (macOS, Apple Silicon)
- Node: v25.5.0
- Repo: `fix/85669-sessions-history-filter-delivery-mirror` @ `4aed94d651` (Bartok9/openclaw fork)

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
git checkout fix/85669-sessions-history-filter-delivery-mirror
CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --prefer-offline
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts \
  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts \
  --reporter=verbose
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/bartokmoltbot/clawd/openclaw

 ✓  agents  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts > sessions tools > uses number (not integer) in tool schemas for Gemini compatibility 5ms
 ✓  agents  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts > sessions tools > sessions_list forwards mailbox filters and includes messages 5ms
 ✓  agents  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts > sessions tools > derives mailbox previews only after agent visibility filtering 2ms
 ✓  agents  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts > sessions tools > sessions_list resolves transcriptPath from agent state dir for multi-store listings 0ms
 ✓  agents  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts > sessions tools > sessions_history filters tool messages by default 1ms
 ✓  agents  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts > sessions tools > sessions_history filters delivery-mirror and gateway-injected messages unconditionally 1ms
 ✓  agents  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts > sessions tools > sessions_history caps oversized payloads and strips heavy fields 10ms
 ✓  agents  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts > sessions tools > sessions_history enforces a hard byte cap even when a single message is huge 1ms
 ✓  agents  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts > sessions tools > sessions_history sets contentRedacted when sensitive data is redacted 3ms
 ✓  agents  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts > sessions tools > sessions_history sets both contentRedacted and contentTruncated independently 0ms
 ✓  agents  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts > sessions tools > sessions_history resolves sessionId inputs 0ms
 ✓  agents  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts > sessions tools > sessions_history errors on missing sessionId 0ms
 ✓  agents  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts > sessions tools > sessions_send supports fire-and-forget and wait 15ms
 ✓  agents  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts > sessions tools > sessions_send resolves sessionId inputs 0ms
 ✓  agents  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts > sessions tools > sessions_send runs ping-pong then announces 7ms
 ✓  agents  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts > sessions tools > sessions_send keeps delayed requester replies alive after a wait timeout 7ms
 ✓  agents  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts > sessions tools > sessions_send reroutes run-scoped active deliveries when transcript steering is rejected 2ms
 ✓  agents  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts > sessions tools > sessions_send preserves active delivery when transcript commit wait is unsupported 1ms
 ✓  agents  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts > sessions tools > sessions_send reports run-scoped fallback admission failures 0ms
 ✓  agents  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts > sessions tools > sessions_send preserves terminal timeouts without starting A2A 0ms
 ✓  agents  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts > sessions tools > sessions_send skips duplicate A2A delivery for waited parent-owned native subagents 0ms
 ✓  agents  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts > sessions tools > sessions_send preserves threadId when announce target is hydrated via sessions.list 7ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  22 passed (22)
   Start at  17:14:56
   Duration  4.11s (transform 2.85s, setup 219ms, import 3.73s, tests 72ms, environment 0ms)
```

**Observed result after fix:**

The new test `sessions_history filters delivery-mirror and gateway-injected messages unconditionally` (line 6 in the run output) exercises the real `sessions_history` tool with a fixture transcript containing both real assistant messages and `delivery-mirror`/`gateway-injected` entries. It verifies the output across **both** code paths: `includeTools=false` (the default) and `includeTools=true`. In both cases the real assistant turns are preserved and the `delivery-mirror`/`gateway-injected` entries are absent. Before this patch the tool returned all entries verbatim and the dashboard / other-agent callers saw doubled assistant turns. The pre-existing 21 tests (sessions_list filters, sessions_history other guards, sessions_send delivery flows) all continue to pass — no regression in the surrounding tool surface.

**What was not tested:**

- End-to-end dashboard rendering of a deduplicated session (this is a server-side tool change verified at the tool boundary)
- Manual gateway loopback (`tools-invoke-http`) — the tool entrypoint is the same code path covered by the test
- The `_historyCallCount` lint warning noted in the PR description is pre-existing in the file and unrelated to this PR's scope

PR surface: Source +116, Tests +97. Total +213 across 5 files.

target.title: fix(mattermost): preserve DM reply context metadata
target.body:
- Problem: Mattermost DM replies were missing reply-target context in the inbound payload, so the agent could not reliably see the quoted/replied-to message body or sender in direct-message reply flows.
- Why it matters: this makes DM reply handling weaker than group/thread handling and degrades reply-aware behavior exactly where humans expect “reply to this message” to carry context.
- What changed: fetch the replied-to Mattermost post in DM reply flows, resolve `ReplyToBody` + `ReplyToSender`, cache the lookup briefly, and add focused regression tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this PR does **not** change Mattermost DM threading policy, final delivery behavior, preview streaming, edited-post wakeups, or message-tool thread/media handling.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #65729

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Mattermost DM replies now carry reply-target message body/sender metadata into the inbound context when a reply target post can be resolved.
- This improves reply-aware behavior in DM reply flows without changing defaults or config.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - Adds a bounded Mattermost REST lookup for the replied-to post when handling DM replies. Risk is limited extra API traffic; mitigated by only querying when a reply target exists and by a short in-memory post cache.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local source checkout / worktree
- Model/provider: n/a (channel integration fix)
- Integration/channel (if any): Mattermost
- Relevant config (redacted): DM-capable Mattermost account, reply target post id present in inbound post/root context

### Steps

1. Receive a Mattermost direct-message post that is itself a reply to an earlier DM post.
2. Let the Mattermost monitor build the inbound context for the agent turn.
3. Inspect the generated inbound payload / regression tests.

### Expected

- `ReplyToBody` and `ReplyToSender` are populated from the replied-to DM post when resolvable.

### Actual

- Before this fix, DM reply flows did not resolve the replied-to post, so reply-target metadata was absent.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification

What was personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - reply-target post lookup path added via `fetchMattermostPost()`
  - reply context hydration helper behavior for bot-authored and user-authored replied-to posts
  - unresolved reply-target handling and safe fallback behavior
  - rebased integration against current `main` with clean conflict resolution
- Edge cases checked:
  - reply target equals current post → skipped
  - unresolved/missing reply target → returns empty context with explicit lookup-failure logging
  - cache growth bounded by pruning expired entries and max-size eviction
- What was **not** verified:
  - live Mattermost workspace end-to-end behavior against a real DM conversation

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Failure Recovery

- How to disable/revert quickly: revert commits `5ac4c3f5a42` and `5a658cc7973`
- Files to restore:
  - `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/client.ts`
  - `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/client.test.ts`
  - `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.ts`
  - `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/reply-context.ts`
  - `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.reply-context.test.ts`
- Known bad symptoms to watch for:
  - unexpected extra REST lookups on DM replies
  - incorrect quoted-sender attribution
  - missing reply metadata where a valid reply target exists

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: extra Mattermost API fetch per uncached DM reply target
  - Mitigation: lookup only when a reply target exists, short TTL cache, expired-entry pruning, bounded cache size
- Risk: reviewers may assume this fixes broader Mattermost DM/thread parity bugs
  - Mitigation: scope boundary is explicit; this PR references #65729 as related but does not claim to close it

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Mattermost DM replies were missing reply-target context (`ReplyToBody` / `ReplyToSender`) in the inbound payload, so reply-aware behavior in direct-message reply flows lost the quoted message context.

Real environment tested: Real local OpenClaw source checkout/worktree on Linux, rebased onto current upstream `main`, using the actual PR branch and fork push flow.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```text
git diff --check
git show --stat --oneline -1 HEAD
git push --force-with-lease fork HEAD:fix/mattermost-dm-reply-context
```
Evidence after fix:
```text
$ git diff --check
Finished in 205ms on 5 files using 2 threads.

$ git show --stat --oneline -1 HEAD
02c829beae4 style(mattermost): satisfy monitor boolean lint rules
5ac4c3f5a42 fix(mattermost): address reply context review notes
5a658cc7973 fix(mattermost): preserve DM reply context metadata

$ git push --force-with-lease fork HEAD:fix/mattermost-dm-reply-context
To https://github.com/tradmangh/openclaw.git
   5ac4c3f5a42..02c829beae4  HEAD -> fix/mattermost-dm-reply-context
```
Observed result after fix: The Mattermost reply-context change is rebased cleanly onto current main, conflict-free, review notes addressed, and the updated branch is pushed successfully. Remaining failing CI is not indicating a Mattermost reply-context logic regression; the currently visible typecheck failure is in unrelated Matrix test code.

What was not tested: Live end-to-end verification against a real Mattermost DM conversation was not completed in this environment.
target.author: tradmangh

PR surface: Source +346, Tests +371, Other +45. Total +762 across 9 files.

target.title: fix(gateway): render codex inline media once
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex-harness replies that emitted local `MEDIA:` image directives could fail to become trusted outgoing media, leaving inline images text-only or unavailable to WebChat/Control UI.
- Why it matters: Agents using the Codex harness should be able to return generated/local images inline without requiring channel-specific manual workarounds, and users should not see duplicate assistant turns for one image reply.
- What changed: Trust structured local media markers from non-external tool results, while preserving the existing registered-tool trust checks; replace the latest raw assistant `MEDIA:` transcript turn with the normalized media-bearing message instead of appending a second assistant image turn.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This does not change external MCP tool trust, local media root serving checks, model prompting, ComfyUI plugin behavior, or any provider/network API.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

No matching upstream issue was found. Searches checked: `inline image`, `inline media`, `Codex harness image`, `MEDIA: image`, `assistant-media`, `managed media`, `Control UI image`, and duplicate image/WebChat terms.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The Codex/AppServer path can persist the model-authored assistant reply as raw text containing `MEDIA:<local path>` while the gateway reply payload carries the parsed/normalized media URL. The WebChat transcript bridge treated those as separate assistant replies and appended the normalized media message after the raw directive turn.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing media tests covered parsing and directive stripping, but did not cover the Codex/AppServer split where the raw transcript text and normalized media payload represent the same reply.
- Contributing context (if known): Local media trust was tied to registered/built-in tool-name trust. Workspace tools that return a structured trusted local media marker needed a generic non-external trust path, still leaving external MCP results untrusted and downstream local-root serving checks intact.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - Non-external structured `trustedLocalMedia` tool results keep local media URLs, while external tool results with the same marker do not.
  - A raw assistant transcript reply containing `MEDIA:<local path>` is replaced by the normalized outgoing media message when the reply payload carries equivalent media, instead of producing two assistant messages.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests hit the two affected seams directly: tool-result media trust filtering and WebChat transcript persistence/replacement.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing directive/media tests covered stripping `MEDIA:` directives into outgoing media blocks, but not replacement of a raw persisted Codex/AppServer assistant turn.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A; new regression coverage is included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex-harness agents can render trusted local image replies inline in WebChat/Control UI without leaving a raw `MEDIA:` line behind or showing a duplicate text-plus-image assistant reply.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text]
                              -> [gateway normalized media reply appended]
                              -> [duplicate assistant turns]

After:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text detected]
                              -> [latest assistant turn replaced with normalized media blocks]
                              -> [single inline image reply]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

Security notes:

- Structured local media trust is ignored for external MCP tool results.
- Registered-tool trust behavior remains in place for built-in/trusted tools.
- The downstream managed-media pipeline still gates local file serving against allowed local media roots before exposing any media URL.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local development workspace; Windows gateway hot-patch was separately smoke-tested before PR preparation.
- Runtime/container: Node local workspace.
- Model/provider: Codex harness path observed with `gpt-5.5` during manual smoke testing.
- Integration/channel (if any): WebChat/Control UI transcript handling and Codex harness agent media payloads.
- Relevant config (redacted): Codex harness enabled; no secrets required for the local unit/seam regression tests.

### Steps

1. Run a Codex-harness agent reply that emits a local image as `MEDIA:<local path>`.
2. Observe the persisted assistant transcript turn and outgoing gateway media payload.
3. Open WebChat/Control UI for the same session.

### Expected

- The reply appears once as an inline image-bearing assistant message.
- The raw `MEDIA:` directive is not shown to the user.
- Local media from external tool results is not trusted solely because it carries a structured marker.

### Actual

- Before this fix, the raw `MEDIA:` assistant text could remain in the transcript and the normalized media message could be appended as a second assistant reply.
- Workspace tool local media markers were not accepted through a structured non-external trust path.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Passing local verification on the rebased branch:

```text
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts
# 42 passed

OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PROJECT_SHARDS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
# 132 passed
```

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - Rebased the fix onto current `openclaw/openclaw` `main`.
  - Verified the agent media trust tests pass.
  - Verified the gateway directive/WebChat media transcript tests pass.
  - Verified TypeScript core and core-test typechecks pass after the upstream rebase.
  - Earlier manual WebChat/Control UI smoke testing confirmed a Codex inline image rendered as one message rather than a duplicate text-plus-image pair.
- Edge cases checked:
  - External tool results with `trustedLocalMedia: true` remain untrusted for local media URLs.
  - Codex/AppServer raw path vs normalized `file://` payload equivalence is handled by replacing the latest raw media directive turn.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full `pnpm build` after the rebase, because this local shell did not have `pnpm` on `PATH`; targeted tests and core typecheck were run directly with repository scripts.
  - Fresh Windows Control UI visual smoke from this exact rebased commit; the equivalent runtime hot-patch was tested before PR preparation.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

N/A for initial PR submission.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Replacing the latest assistant transcript turn could hide an unrelated prior assistant message if detection is too broad.
  - Mitigation: Replacement only runs when the latest leaf is an assistant message containing parseable `MEDIA:` directives and the current reply payload is media-bearing; tests cover the raw Codex/AppServer directive replacement path.
- Risk: Structured local media markers could be abused by external tool results.
  - Mitigation: The marker is ignored for external MCP/tool results, and media serving still requires downstream allowed-root validation.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex/AppServer replies that emit a raw `MEDIA:<local path>` image now normalize into the existing assistant transcript turn instead of leaving raw MEDIA text and appending a duplicate gateway-injected media reply. Current head tested: a046242234bbbdc369730a38fc9b0b2d1be9d211.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw PR checkout on Linux, Node v24.13.1, real session transcript files, local media file fixtures, and the gateway chat transcript/media rewrite path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: From the PR checkout after applying the ClawSweeper review fixes, ran `git diff --check`, `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`, and `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
$ PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.6 /tmp/openclaw-pr-resolve-20260517

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  77 passed (77)
   Start at  00:34:46
   Duration  12.92s (transform 2.53s, setup 291ms, import 6.75s, tests 5.52s, environment 0ms)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts\n\n RUN  v4.1.6 /tmp/openclaw-pr-resolve-20260517\n\n Test Files  1 passed (1)\n      Tests  78 passed (78)\n   Duration  12.41s\n\n[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 23.72s
```

- Observed result after fix: The focused gateway transcript/media suite passed on current head, including the new parser-parity regression for an unquoted local MEDIA path containing spaces, coverage that preserves captions while stripping raw `MEDIA:` directives, refuses to rewrite an older matching media turn after a newer assistant turn, and avoids a second message-level transcript update after the file-level rewrite event. The browser proof rendered the normalized assistant image reply as a single bubble with one image and no raw `MEDIA:` text.
Browser-rendered Control UI proof on current head:


target.title: feat(ui): replace textarea with CodeMirror 6 JSON editor in raw config view
target.body:
## Summary

Replace the plain `<textarea>` for raw config editing with a proper CodeMirror 6 JSON5 editor component.

### Changes

**New file: `ui/src/ui/views/config-editor.ts`**
- CodeMirror 6 editor with JSON5 language mode (handles comments, unquoted keys, trailing commas)
- Syntax highlighting, fold gutter, line numbers, active line
- Real-time JSON5 linting with error markers (red dot in gutter)
- Format/prettify button (JSON5-aware via `JSON5.parse`)
- Read-only mode via `Compartment` (live toggle without recreating editor)
- Dark/light theme via `themeCompartment`
- Full dark theme: `#1f2937` background, `#e5e7eb` text
- `errorOffset()` handles both JSON5 ("at line:col") and JSON ("position N") error formats

**`ui/src/ui/views/config.ts`**
- Replace `<textarea>` with `<config-editor>` component
- Fix dark mode binding: `?dark` now correctly follows `system` mode via `prefersLightScheme()`

**`ui/src/ui/views/config.browser.test.ts`**
- Update test: `<textarea>` → `<config-editor>` + CustomEvent("change") instead of native input event

**`ui/package.json`** — new dependencies
- `codemirror-json5`, `json5`, `lezer-json5`
- `@codemirror/{autocomplete,commands,lang-json,language,lint,state,view}`

**`CHANGELOG.md`**
- One-liner entry under `### Changes`

### Motivation

OpenClaw raw config uses JSON5 format (comments, trailing commas). Plain `<textarea>` can't validate or highlight it. The new editor provides real-time linting, format button, and proper dark-mode system-theme following.

### Test plan

- [x] ESLint / TypeScript checks pass
- [x] Config editor renders in light and dark modes
- [x] System dark mode correctly triggers dark theme
- [x] JSON5 linting marks errors with red gutter dots
- [x] Format button prettifies valid JSON5 content
- [x] Read-only mode disables editing
- [x] Undo/redo (Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y) works
- [x] Browser test updated and passes

Closes #56401

target.title: fix(agents): pause yielded main-session runs
target.body:
## Summary

Extend the subagent yield-pause fix from [6f3b5f86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/6f3b5f8666f3a3877b62d9019b8842455fdf62e1) to top-level (non-subagent) sessions.

When a main session ends via the `sessions_yield` tool, the runner already emits a lifecycle event with `yielded: true` and `livenessState: "paused"`. The runner's terminal lifecycle metadata, the agent-job snapshot cache, and the agent.wait dedupe entry all propagate those flags generically, so subagent and non-subagent runs benefit equally from those parts of 6f3b5f86.

The gap is in `gateway/session-lifecycle-state.ts`. `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` does not look at the `yielded` flag, so the session entry is persisted with `status: \"done\"` even though the runner has queued a post-yield continuation that drains on the next turn. Dashboards, restart recovery, and follow-up turns then treat the session as finished and race the continuation, producing the in-context overflow and out-of-order events the user reports.

## Why the same fix needs to apply to the top-level session

The subagent registry tracks active subagent runs in memory and uses `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` to keep yielded entries in a paused state instead of marking them complete. The main session has no equivalent in-memory registry; its lifecycle state is persisted directly through `persistGatewaySessionLifecycleEvent` into the on-disk session entry. The gap shows up in three places:

1. The persisted session entry says `status: \"done\"`, so `sessions_list`, `sessions.changed` broadcasts, and dashboard UI all show the session as finished.
2. `main-session-restart-recovery` is keyed off `entry.abortedLastRun` and `entry.status === \"running\"`. With status=done it leaves the entry alone, but on a clean restart-mid-yield the yielded session can also end up `abortedLastRun=true` (the cleaned-lock path), at which point recovery would force a `[System] Your previous turn was interrupted...` resume that races the queued continuation.
3. A new user message arrives at a session that is internally paused but externally \"done\" — the gateway dispatches a fresh agent.run, and the queued steered yield-interrupt drains alongside the new user input, producing out-of-order assistant text.

## Shape of the change

- Recognize the lifecycle `yielded` flag in `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` and derive `status: \"paused\"` with `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` instead of `done`. Yielded short-circuits before the `aborted`/`stopReason` branches so partial classifications cannot mask the paused state.
- Clear `pauseReason` on the next lifecycle `start` event so a fresh run reactivates the session, mirroring how `subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts` clears `pauseReason` on completion.
- Add `\"paused\"` to `SessionRunStatus` in both `gateway/session-utils.types.ts` and `agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts`, and add `pauseReason?: \"sessions_yield\"` to `SessionEntry` and `GatewaySessionRow`.
- Surface `pauseReason` on the `sessions.changed` broadcast snapshot and the `loadGatewaySessionRow` builder so consumers (UI, sessions_list tool) see the paused state.
- Tighten `isTerminalSessionStatus` so the type predicate stays accurate after \"paused\" joins the union (paused is not terminal).
- Skip `main-session-restart-recovery` for entries with `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` so they are not re-prompted with the generic interrupted-turn message.

The runner-level changes (yieldDetected -> livenessState=paused/yielded=true) and the gateway agent-job/agent-wait-dedupe propagation that 6f3b5f86 already added are unchanged. This PR plugs the missing seam between those generic signals and the persisted session entry.

## Test coverage

- `src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts`:
  - `marks yielded lifecycle end events as paused with sessions_yield reason` (persisted patch).
  - `treats yielded as paused even when aborted is set` (yield short-circuit).
  - `clears pauseReason when a fresh run starts on a paused session` (start-phase reactivation).
  - Existing terminal-mapping tests updated to include explicit `pauseReason: undefined` on non-yield ends.
- `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`:
  - `skips sessions paused via sessions_yield instead of forcing a resume`.
  - `does not mark sessions with sessions_yield pauseReason as restart-aborted from cleaned locks`.

## Local validation

- `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` — clean.
- `pnpm test src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts src/config/sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.create-event.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts` — all green (235 tests).
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check`, `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs`, `pnpm format:docs:check`, `pnpm check:changelog-attributions` — all clean on changed files.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI green on \`fix(agents): pause yielded main-session runs\`.
- [ ] Manual smoke: trigger `sessions_yield` from a main-session conversation; confirm the session entry persists `status: paused` and `pauseReason: sessions_yield`, and that a follow-up turn flips back to `running` with `pauseReason: undefined`.
- [ ] Restart-recovery smoke: kill the gateway while a main session is paused via yield; confirm restart-recovery skips it instead of re-prompting with the interrupted-turn message.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Before this PR, when a top-level (non-subagent) embedded `openclaw` run ended via the `sessions_yield` tool, the runner emitted `yielded=true` and `livenessState=paused` but the gateway treated it as a clean completion and wrote `status=done`. The session displayed as finished while awaiting a queued post-yield continuation, racing dashboards, restart-recovery, and follow-up turns. After this PR, the lifecycle persistence derives `status=paused` with `pauseReason=sessions_yield`, restart-recovery skips paused sessions, and `SessionRunStatus` adds `"paused"` so consumers can distinguish a yielded run from a completed one.
- **Real environment tested**: live OpenClaw gateway on rh-bot.lan, pid 65734, build SHA 23804e610c (upgrade-v2026.5.28 with this PR cherry-picked). The deployed `dist/server-chat-*.js` and `dist/main-session-restart-recovery-*.js` chunks carry the new `pauseReason`/`sessions_yield` logic; the proof script imports and executes those exact bundle bytes.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  # On rh-bot.lan, confirm deployed bundles carry the PR logic:
  grep -c 'sessions_yield\|pauseReason' \
    /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js
  # -> 1 (the new shouldSkipMainRecovery branch)

  grep -c 'sessions_yield\|pauseReason' \
    /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js
  # -> non-zero (new deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot branches)

  # Drive the deployed code with three scenarios:
  node /tmp/proof-75662-amittell.mjs
  ```
  Proof script (`/tmp/proof-75662-amittell.mjs`) extracts the deployed
  `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` from `server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js` via
  `vm.runInContext`, dynamically imports `main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js`
  to grab the exported `markRestartAbortedMainSessions`, seeds a temp
  state-dir with one yielded and one non-yielded `running` entry, and asserts
  three behaviors:
  1. Lifecycle `phase=end` with `yielded=true` -> `status=paused`,
     `pauseReason=sessions_yield`, `abortedLastRun=false`.
  2. Same entry passed to restart-recovery is SKIPPED (no `abortedLastRun`
     write, `updatedAt` unchanged).
  3. Sibling running entry without `pauseReason` is marked
     `abortedLastRun=true` (existing behavior preserved).
  Plus a guard check: lifecycle `phase=error` with `yielded=true` returns
  `status=failed` with NO `pauseReason` leak.
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  === PR #75662 real-behavior proof ===
  host=rh-bot.lan pid=84884 node=v25.8.2
  dist=/Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist

  [1] lifecycle bundle: /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js
  [1a] yielded terminal -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"paused","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false,"pauseReason":"sessions_yield"}
  [1a] OK: paused status carries pauseReason=sessions_yield
  [1b] non-yielded terminal -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"done","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false}
  [1b] OK: non-yielded run preserves status=done with no pauseReason
  [1c] error+yielded -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"failed","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false}
  [1c] OK: error phase masks yielded marker, no pauseReason leak

  [2] seeded /var/folders/cn/.../state/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  [2] recovery bundle: /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js
  [2] exports: i, n, r, t
  [main-session-restart-recovery] marked 1 interrupted main session(s) for restart recovery (pr-75662-proof)
  [2] mark result: {"marked":1,"skipped":1}
  [2] yielded entry after: {"sessionId":"sess-yielded","status":"running","pauseReason":"sessions_yield","startedAt":...,"updatedAt":1780285519789}
  [2] normal entry after:  {"sessionId":"sess-normal","status":"running","startedAt":...,"updatedAt":1780285521266,"abortedLastRun":true}
  [2a] OK: yielded session was skipped (no abortedLastRun, no updatedAt bump)
  [2b] OK: non-yielded running session was marked abortedLastRun=true

  === ALL PROOFS PASSED ===
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: All three scenarios pass on the deployed bundle. A yielded terminal event produces `status=paused`/`pauseReason=sessions_yield` and is skipped by restart recovery (no `abortedLastRun` mutation, no `updatedAt` bump). A non-yielded terminal event still produces `status=done` with no `pauseReason` (existing behavior preserved). An error phase masks the `yielded` flag and reports `status=failed` with no `pauseReason` leak, matching the gating comment in `resolveTerminalStatus`.
- **Targeted tests**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts` -> 22 passed + 15 passed (37/37). Broader: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts` -> 172/172 passed. `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --only=core --threads=4` -> clean.
- **What was not tested**: End-to-end live channel trigger (sending a real Telegram message that drives `sessions_yield` to completion); this is exercised by the existing vitest coverage in `pi-embedded-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts` rather than reproduced live on rh-bot.


## Live exact-head capture (2026-06-12): found and fixed an emit-side gap

Running the requested live scenario on an isolated sandbox gateway (loopback :28795, fresh `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, Control UI webchat, native `openai-completions` lane with a tool-calling model) surfaced a real defect in this PR's original head: **the paused state never persisted in live runs, on any lane.** A store probe polling every 0.3s across four real `sessions_spawn` + `sessions_yield` turns at head `be96a422ff` captured the persisted row going `running -> killed` with `abortedLastRun: true` at yield time -- the exact pre-PR failure this PR exists to prevent.

**Root cause (probe-instrumented):** `sessions_yield` aborts the attempt mid-stream (`runAbortController.abort("sessions_yield")`), and the terminal lifecycle event can be emitted from the abort teardown before `run.ts` post-processing writes the `yielded` meta. The bundle-level instrumentation captured the failing ordering directly:

```
emitLifecycleTerminal state.yielded = undefined  aborted = false   <- event emitted first
meta.yielded set -> false                                          <- run.ts wrote 551ms later
```

The event reaches `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` carrying `aborted` without `yielded`, and `resolveTerminalStatus` correctly resolves killed. The projection/recovery/UI logic this PR adds was never the problem; the emit side never fed it. The unit tests passed because they fabricate events that already carry `yielded: true`.

**Fix (in `a46ba508b8`):** `onYield` now records `yielded` through the subscription's terminal lifecycle meta channel at tool time, before the abort, using the same late-binding pattern the yield abort itself uses (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`). A regression test pins the handler half: an end event with `state.yielded` set and `aborted` resolved from the `isTerminalAborted` fallback still carries `yielded` (`src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts`).

**Post-fix live proof, end to end (same sandbox, instrumented bundle):**

```
10:27:40.065  [PROBE] meta.yielded set -> true              (onYield records at tool time)
10:27:40.423  [PROBE] emitLifecycleTerminal state.yielded = true   (event carries it)
10:27:4x      store: status=paused pauseReason=sessions_yield abortedLastRun=false
~10:28        Sessions Control UI: main session "Paused", subagent "Live"
10:28:1x      gateway KILLED while paused -> store unchanged (paused, ab=false)
10:28:3x      gateway restarted -> recovery skips the paused session;
              no "[System] Your previous turn was interrupted" prompt in the chat
10:29:20      subagent (orphan-recovered post-restart) completes -> announce drains
              the queued continuation in order -> running, pauseReason cleared
10:30:48      continuation finishes -> done, abortedLastRun=false
```

Reproduced again on a second pass (store probe `paused/sessions_yield` at 15:54-15:56 with closure-identity probes confirming the onYield-time recording). This covers all three asks: visible Paused state, restart skip, and orderly continuation, captured live at the exact head plus the one-commit fix.

**Codex lane now covered too (was a sibling-lane gap, now fixed in this PR).** The Codex app-server harness lane emits its own terminal lifecycle event (`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` `emitLifecycleTerminal`) and was dropping the yield meta: the `phase:"end"` event carried only `phase`/`aborted`, never `yielded`, so the shared projection (`resolveYielded`) persisted Codex-backed yields as done/killed. The fix forwards `{yielded, livenessState:"paused", stopReason:"end_turn"}` on that event when `result.yieldDetected`, exactly as the native runner does, gated on the flag the `sessions_yield` tool already sets via `onYield -> onYieldDetected`. The Codex lifecycle event flows through the same `onAgentEvent -> server-chat -> persistGatewaySessionLifecycleEvent -> resolveYielded` path as the native lane.

Proven live on the Codex lane: a real `openai/gpt-5.5` turn through the Codex app-server (ChatGPT OAuth) that calls `sessions_yield` now persists `status=paused`, `pauseReason=sessions_yield` (was `done` pre-fix), and the subagent then drains the queued continuation in order back to `done`. An integration test (`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.yield-lifecycle.test.ts`) asserts the Codex lifecycle end event carries `yielded:true`/`livenessState:"paused"`/`stopReason:"end_turn"` on yield and omits them on a normal turn (verified red without the fix).

Codex protocol behavior checked against the sibling `../codex` source (HEAD `b8aea2340`): `codex-rs/app-server-protocol/schema/typescript/ServerRequest.ts:19` defines the `{ "method": "item/tool/call", id, params: DynamicToolCallParams }` app-server -> client request, which is how the app-server invokes the OpenClaw `sessions_yield` dynamic tool (setting `yieldDetected`); turn lifecycle requests (`turn/start`/`turn/steer`/`turn/interrupt`) are at `ClientRequest.ts:90`. This change adds no new protocol dependency -- it forwards an OpenClaw-side flag onto OpenClaw's own lifecycle event.

target.title: feat(plugins): add session followup turn API and gateway-restart extension
target.body:
## Summary

Add a new `runtime.followup.enqueueFollowupTurn()` method to the plugin runtime, enabling plugins to schedule proactive agent turns for any session — including cold sessions with no active user interaction.

> **Supersedes #60951** — rebased onto latest main, all CI issues resolved, bug fixes included.

## Problem

Plugins have no way to inject a user-role message into an existing session and trigger an agent turn. The available primitives (`enqueueSystemEvent`, `subagent.run`) either don't initiate turns or create new sessions instead of continuing the original conversation. This blocks use cases like:

- **Post-restart session resume** — an agent restarts its own gateway and needs to automatically continue working after the restart completes
- **Model backend switching** — an agent swaps inference backends (e.g., switching between local LLMs on a single-GPU workstation) and needs to resume the session on the new model with full context
- **Proactive plugin notifications** — a service detects an external event and needs to wake a sleeping session
- **Deferred task completion** — a plugin schedules follow-up work after a long-running background operation

## Solution

### Core API: `runtime.followup.enqueueFollowupTurn()`

A new `followup` namespace on `PluginRuntimeCore` that:

1. **Reconstructs a full `FollowupRun`** from the session store via `buildFollowupRunForSession()` — resolving agent identity, workspace, provider/model, session file, and delivery context from the persisted `SessionEntry`.

2. **Enqueues the run** into the existing followup queue infrastructure with preserved queue mode (respects `mode` of any existing queue for the session key, defaults to `"followup"`).

3. **Handles cold-start drain** by detecting whether the session's followup queue is already draining. If not (cold session), creates a fresh followup runner with preloaded session metadata and a no-op typing controller, then kicks the drain loop.

```typescript
// Plugin usage
const enqueued = await api.runtime.followup.enqueueFollowupTurn({
  sessionKey: "agent:my-agent:telegram:direct:12345",
  prompt: "Background task complete. Here are the results...",
  source: "my-plugin",
});
```

### Bundled Extension: `gateway-restart`

A reference implementation that demonstrates the followup turn API:

- **Tool** (`gateway_restart`): Agent calls this to run optional allowlisted pre-commands, write a restart marker (only after commands succeed), then exit the process. Systemd (or equivalent) auto-restarts the gateway.

- **Service** (`gateway-restart-watcher`): On startup, checks for the restart marker. If found, calls `enqueueFollowupTurn()` to resume the original session with a completion message. Marker is deleted only after successful enqueue — preserved for retry on failure.

Disabled by default (`enabledByDefault: false`). Opt-in via `plugins.entries.gateway-restart.enabled: true`.

## Files Changed

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/queue/build-followup-run.ts` | New: cold-session FollowupRun builder with session store resolution |
| `src/plugins/runtime/runtime-followup.ts` | New: plugin runtime followup namespace with hot/cold session detection |
| `src/plugins/runtime/types-core.ts` | Add `followup` type to `PluginRuntimeCore` |
| `src/plugins/runtime/index.ts` | Wire `createRuntimeFollowup()` |
| `extensions/gateway-restart/index.ts` | New: gateway-restart plugin (tool + watcher service) |
| `extensions/gateway-restart/openclaw.plugin.json` | New: plugin manifest |
| `extensions/gateway-restart/package.json` | New: package metadata |
| `test/helpers/plugins/plugin-runtime-mock.ts` | Add `followup` to mock |
| `**/**.test.ts` | 24 unit tests across 3 test files |

## Bug Fixes (vs #60951)

1. **Marker written after pre-commands** — prevents stale markers when a pre-command fails
2. **Marker deleted after enqueue confirmed** — preserves marker for retry if `enqueueFollowupTurn()` returns false
3. **Hot session guard** — `rememberFollowupDrainCallback` only registers for cold sessions; existing hot-session drain runners are not overwritten
4. **Queue mode preservation** — `enqueueFollowupRun` respects existing queue mode instead of forcing `"followup"`
5. **Session metadata preload** — cold followup runners receive preloaded session store and entry for accounting/refresh paths

## Testing

- 24 unit tests across 3 test files (all passing)
- `pnpm check` passes (tsgo + oxlint + all architecture checks)
- Rebased onto latest main
- End-to-end verified on a local workstation running OpenClaw 2026.4.8 with Gemma 4 26B via llama.cpp

## Use Cases This Enables

This API is the foundation for several plugins currently in development:

- **`model-switch`** — seamless switching between local LLMs on single-GPU workstations, with session continuation via `enqueueFollowupTurn()` after the new model is healthy
- **`inference-guard`** — priority-aware request scheduling for single-slot inference (heartbeat/cron defer behind user messages)
- **Self-modifying agents** — agents that update their own config, restart the gateway, and validate changes automatically

## Test plan

- [ ] `pnpm check` passes
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run extensions/gateway-restart/index.test.ts` — 6 tests pass
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run src/plugins/runtime/runtime-followup.test.ts` — 9 tests pass

target.title: fix(qqbot): surface failed media sends
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes the QQBot message adapter so failed media sends no longer look like successful sends with an empty `platformMessageIds` receipt.
- Keeps the QQ C2C media upload routing unchanged after the #92747 maintainer feedback; this PR does not switch one-shot uploads to chunked uploads.
- Treats both known outbound errors and missing QQ platform message ids as send failures, so callers can see the failure instead of receiving a misleading successful receipt.
- This is intentionally a smaller follow-up to #92747: it only fixes the error-propagation boundary that maintainers called useful, while leaving QQ upload contract selection for a separately proven change.

## Linked context

Related #92246

Related #92747

This follows the maintainer feedback on #92747 that the adapter error-propagation improvement is useful but should be separated from the broader C2C upload-route change.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: QQBot media sends that hit an outbound failure are now surfaced as failures instead of successful empty receipts.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw PR branch using the real QQBot channel message adapter, a real QQBot app credential, a local PNG stored under the QQBot-owned media directory, and a redacted invalid C2C target so the request safely reaches QQ's media upload endpoint but cannot deliver to a real user.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Credentialed runtime probe: `env -u QQBOT_CLIENT_SECRET QQBOT_APP_ID='[redacted]' QQBOT_APP_SECRET='[redacted]' OPENCLAW_PROXY_ACTIVE=1 http_proxy='[redacted proxy]' https_proxy='[redacted proxy]' node_modules/.bin/tsx '<temporary local probe script>'`
  - Regression suite: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/api/media.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix (credentialed, redacted runtime output from the real QQBot plugin/media path):

  ```json
  {
    "scenario": "credentialed real QQBot plugin media adapter call through proxy with allowed local png and invalid redacted C2C target",
    "credentialed": true,
    "openclawManagedEnvProxy": true,
    "globalUndiciProxyDispatcher": true,
    "mockedOutbound": false,
    "localMediaFile": true,
    "qqApiAttempted": true,
    "targetRedacted": true,
    "outcome": "rejected",
    "error": "API Error [/v2/users/redacted-invalid-openid-for-proof/files]: invalid request"
  }
  ```

- Evidence after fix (token probe with the same credential and proxy, token redacted):

  ```json
  {
    "scenario": "credentialed QQ token endpoint probe through proxy",
    "credentialed": true,
    "proxyConfigured": true,
    "status": 200,
    "body": "{"access_token":"[redacted-token]","expires_in":"2323"}"
  }
  ```

- Evidence after fix (regression validation): Validation `qqbot-channel-and-media` (pass, exit_code=0, 39982ms):

  ```text
  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-messaging.config.ts
  
   RUN  v4.1.7 [local path redacted]
  
  
   Test Files  3 passed (3)
        Tests  42 passed (42)
     Start at  09:21:58
     Duration  29.53s (transform 26.32s, setup 808ms, import 35.23s, tests 270ms, environment 1ms)
  
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 39.76s
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the real QQBot plugin media adapter call reached QQ's `/v2/users/{openid}/files` media upload endpoint with a credentialed request and rejected with the QQ API error. It did not resolve with `{ messageId: "", receipt: { platformMessageIds: [] } }`.
- What was not tested: no media message was delivered to a real QQ user. The target was intentionally redacted/invalid so the proof could exercise the real QQ API failure path without sending a message to someone.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this proves this PR's bounded failure-propagation behavior through the real plugin/media path and QQ API failure response. It still does not claim to fix or replace the QQ upload route/payload contract from #92246.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the new regression tests failed before the adapter change because the promise resolved with `{ messageId: '', receipt: { platformMessageIds: [] } }` instead of rejecting.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/api/media.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts`

Regression coverage added:

- QQBot media adapter rejects when the lower-level outbound send returns an error.
- QQBot media adapter rejects when the lower-level outbound send returns neither an error nor a QQ platform message id.

The first new regression failed before this fix with a resolved empty receipt instead of a rejected send.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes`)

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

What is the highest-risk area?

- QQBot callers now see media send failures instead of receiving a successful empty receipt.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The change is limited to `extensions/qqbot/src/channel.ts` and its adapter tests.
- The PR does not change C2C upload routing, QQ API payload shape, config, schema, migrations, or channel public APIs.
- Existing QQBot media/API/outbound-dispatch focused tests still pass.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Maintainer review or ClawSweeper re-review of the credentialed runtime proof.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Nothing author-side for this smaller error-propagation fix. A future upload-route fix would still need separate before/after delivery proof and any QQ response details needed to justify changing the upload route or payload contract.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Addresses the #92747 maintainer feedback by splitting out only the adapter error-propagation improvement and dropping the speculative broad C2C chunked upload routing change.
- Addresses ClawSweeper's `mock-only-proof` feedback by adding credentialed runtime proof through the real QQBot plugin adapter, media path, token endpoint, and QQ media upload endpoint.

target.title: fix(agents): keep apostrophes in slash-command prompt-template arguments
target.body:
## Summary

- `parseCommandArgs` (`packages/agent-core/src/harness/prompt-template-arguments.ts`) expands `$ARGUMENTS` / `$@` / `$1..$N` in user prompt templates and custom slash commands. It does shell-style quote parsing with **no escape and no unbalanced-quote recovery**, so a lone `'` (an ordinary apostrophe in English prose: `user's`, `it's`, `don't`) opens a quote that runs to the next apostrophe (or end of string). The apostrophe is dropped and the following whitespace is swallowed into one token. The corrupted tokens then feed `$ARGUMENTS` (`args.join(" ")`) and the positional placeholders, so the model **silently receives a mangled instruction** with no error or warning.
  - `/cmd don't it's broken now` + a `[$1][$2]` template collapses to `ARG1=[dont its] ARG2=[broken]`: both apostrophes dropped, placeholders shifted.
- Reach: shipped and default-on. `AgentSession.prompt()` expands templates by default (`expandPromptTemplates ?? true`) and the **expanded text becomes the user message sent to the model**. Templates are real user `.md` files loaded by `loadPromptTemplates` from `<agentDir>/prompts`, `<cwd>/.openclaw/prompts`, and explicit prompt paths.
- Fix: single-quote handling gets a prose-aware rule. An apostrophe opens a span only when content follows immediately (so a trailing `users'` stays literal) and closes one only when it is not followed by a word character (so a contraction like `it's` stays literal even inside a span); a span is honored only when an opener and a closer pair up. A word-attached apostrophe (`don't`, `foo'bar`) additionally yields to a later standalone span opener (a whitespace-delimited `'`), so a contraction cannot steal the closing quote of a self-delimited phrase that follows: `don't 'quoted text' next` keeps `don't` literal and groups `quoted text`. Double quotes and shell-style single-quote grouping still behave as before (`alpha "beta gamma"`, `--title="two words"`, `foo='bar baz'`, bare concatenation `foo'bar baz'`). Word-character detection is Unicode-aware (`\p{L}`/`\p{N}`), so non-ASCII prose like `José's` is preserved, and an unmatched leading quote (`'review don't fail`) does not latch onto a later contraction.
- One canonical change in `parseCommandArgs`; both consumers (`expandPromptTemplate` in `src/agents/sessions/prompt-templates.ts` and `formatPromptTemplateInvocation` in `packages/agent-core`) benefit with no API change. The separate `src/auto-reply/commands-registry.ts` `parseCommandArgs` (native channel commands) is a different function and is left untouched.

## Tests

- `packages/agent-core/src/harness/prompt-templates.test.ts`: added cases covering apostrophes preserved in parsed tokens and through `$ARGUMENTS` / positional placeholders; multiple contractions in one input; shell-style quoted spans embedded in a token (`--title="two words"`, `foo='bar baz'`); bare embedded single-quote concatenation (`foo'bar baz'`); a plural possessive next to a later quoted span (`fix users' 'quoted text'`); apostrophes after non-ASCII word characters (`José's don't fail`); an unmatched leading quote that must not close on a later contraction (`'review don't fail`); a contraction inside a balanced quoted phrase (`'it's a test'`); and a contraction immediately before a later standalone single-quoted span (`don't 'quoted text' next`, `it's a 'test case' here`). The existing quoted/multiline and placeholder-safety cases are unchanged and green.
- `src/agents/sessions/prompt-templates.test.ts` (`expandPromptTemplate`) unchanged and green.
- Touched suites run locally with the repo runner; type-aware oxlint + oxfmt clean on the touched files.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: a user-authored custom slash command / prompt template invoked with ordinary prose containing an apostrophe silently lost the apostrophe (and, for positional `$N` templates, collapsed arguments and shifted later placeholders), so the model received a corrupted instruction with no error or warning. Covers ASCII contractions, multiple contractions in one input, non-ASCII contractions, an unmatched leading quote, and a contraction immediately before a later single-quoted span.
- Real environment tested: Linux, Node 22.19, exercising the actual production functions `loadPromptTemplates` + `expandPromptTemplate` (the same expansion path `AgentSession.prompt()` uses to build the outbound user message) over a real on-disk prompt directory (`<agentDir>/prompts/*.md`).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: wrote `<tmp>/prompts/cmd.md` = `ARG1=[$1] ARG2=[$2] ARG3=[$3] ARG4=[$4]`; loaded it via `loadPromptTemplates({ cwd, agentDir, promptPaths, includeDefaults: false })`; called `expandPromptTemplate(...)` for each input below; ran the script over the **current PR head** (`07421d6a80db`) and, for the before column, over the `origin/main` parser source with the rest of the path identical.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied live output from running the real `expandPromptTemplate` over the on-disk templates on the current PR head `07421d6a80db`:

  ```
  TEMPLATES LOADED: [ 'cmd' ]
  USER TYPES : /cmd don't it's broken now
  MODEL GETS : ARG1=[don't] ARG2=[it's] ARG3=[broken] ARG4=[now]

  USER TYPES : /cmd --title="two words" next
  MODEL GETS : ARG1=[--title=two words] ARG2=[next] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

  USER TYPES : /cmd José's don't fail
  MODEL GETS : ARG1=[José's] ARG2=[don't] ARG3=[fail] ARG4=[]

  USER TYPES : /cmd 'review don't fail
  MODEL GETS : ARG1=['review] ARG2=[don't] ARG3=[fail] ARG4=[]

  USER TYPES : /cmd 'it's a test' next
  MODEL GETS : ARG1=[it's a test] ARG2=[next] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

  USER TYPES : /cmd don't 'quoted text' next
  MODEL GETS : ARG1=[don't] ARG2=[quoted text] ARG3=[next] ARG4=[]

  USER TYPES : /cmd it's a 'test case' here
  MODEL GETS : ARG1=[it's] ARG2=[a] ARG3=[test case] ARG4=[here]
  ```

- Observed result after fix: apostrophes survive end to end across contractions, multiple contractions, non-ASCII prose, an unmatched leading quote, a contraction inside a balanced quoted phrase (`'it's a test'` groups to one argument), and a contraction immediately before a later single-quoted span (`don't 'quoted text'` keeps `don't` literal and groups `quoted text`); mid-token shell quoting (`--title="two words"`) still groups into a single argument. The model receives the exact instruction the user typed.
- What was not tested: the separate native-channel `src/auto-reply/commands-registry.ts` `parseCommandArgs` (a different function, untouched); a fully wired gateway round-trip through a live provider (the proof drives the exact expansion function that produces the user message rather than issuing a model call).
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: none specific; the expansion is deterministic and reproduced on demand.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the same script over the `origin/main` parser (unpatched) corrupts every prose case (only the already-balanced mid-token span survives):

  ```
  TEMPLATES LOADED: [ 'cmd' ]
  USER TYPES : /cmd don't it's broken now
  MODEL GETS : ARG1=[dont its] ARG2=[broken] ARG3=[now] ARG4=[]

  USER TYPES : /cmd --title="two words" next
  MODEL GETS : ARG1=[--title=two words] ARG2=[next] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

  USER TYPES : /cmd José's don't fail
  MODEL GETS : ARG1=[Josés dont] ARG2=[fail] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

  USER TYPES : /cmd 'review don't fail
  MODEL GETS : ARG1=[review dont] ARG2=[fail] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

  USER TYPES : /cmd 'it's a test' next
  MODEL GETS : ARG1=[its] ARG2=[a] ARG3=[test next] ARG4=[]

  USER TYPES : /cmd don't 'quoted text' next
  MODEL GETS : ARG1=[dont quoted] ARG2=[text next] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

  USER TYPES : /cmd it's a 'test case' here
  MODEL GETS : ARG1=[its a test] ARG2=[case here] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]
  ```

target.title: fix(config): compare size-drop guard against canonical config

## Summary

Fixes #71865.

- compare config size-drop protection against the canonical previous source config for valid existing files, instead of raw editor-format bytes
- keep the raw-byte fallback for invalid existing configs so malformed/clobbered files remain protected

target.title: feat(claude): add claude-bridge app-server harness extension
target.body:
## Summary

Inspiration: https://openclaw.ai/blog/openai-models-in-openclaw-done-right; achieve parity for Claude

OpenClaw currently has no first-class harness for Anthropic models. Anthropic users either fall back to a generic provider client (no native tool execution, no extended-thinking awareness, no per-turn rate-limit surfacing) or run a Claude harness out-of-tree with no contract for OpenClaw's plugin-SDK seams.

The codex harness already ships in-tree with the same shape we want — `extensions/codex/src/app-server/` is a codex-app-server JSON-RPC client over stdio. We have a working Anthropic-side equivalent built and running in production on the author's setup; opening it for maintainer steer before doing more SDK work on top.

**What is the intended outcome?**

- Bundled extension `@openclaw/claude` registers `claude-bridge` as a harness option in the model picker.
- At turn-start, the extension spawns an `openclaw-claude-bridge` subprocess and speaks the codex-shaped JSON-RPC protocol over stdio, directly wrapping the latest claude agent SDK. The server (currently published as `@zeroaltitude/openclaw-claude-bridge` — fork-preview path; would migrate to `@openclaw/openclaw-claude-bridge` if the maintainers republish it) handles Anthropic API I/O, rate-limit surfacing, image-payload sanitization, and plugin-thread-config persistence.
- Bridge-side handles event projection into OpenClaw's notification/accumulator surface, transcript mirroring with idempotency keys, dynamic-tool aggregate output cap, vision-tools de-duplication, and `/claude` slash commands.
- See `extensions/claude/README.md` for the full feature map.

**What is intentionally out of scope?**

- Republishing the server under `@openclaw/openclaw-claude-bridge` — needs maintainer decision; the `@zeroaltitude` scope dependency is the current placeholder.
- Migrating to OpenClaw's standard plugin-SDK provider hooks beyond what `extensions/codex/` already uses. The bridge follows codex's pattern verbatim where applicable.
- Anything outside the harness bridge surface.

**What does success look like?** Anthropic users get parity with codex users: native tool dispatch, rate-limit-aware errors, extended-thinking-friendly transcript mirroring, harness-level event projection. No regressions to existing harnesses.

**What should reviewers focus on?**

1. **Scope concern — am I taking too much core?** The branch touches `src/plugins/command-registration.ts`, `src/plugins/current-plugin-metadata-snapshot.ts`, `src/plugins/agent-tool-result-middleware-types.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.ts`, `src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.ts`, and `src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts`. Each is small and necessary to wire a harness extension, but the contribution guide says "one thing per PR." Happy to split into prep PRs first if maintainers prefer.
2. **External-plugin vs. bundled-extension** — Per CONTRIBUTING.md "most features should be third-party plugins." Open to packaging this entirely as an external plugin instead of `extensions/claude/`, if that is preferred.
3. **Anthropic-API behavior delegated to a separate server repo** — Most of the actual API logic lives in the server (https://github.com/zeroaltitude/openclaw-plugins/tree/main/openclaw-claude/server). Reviewers will probably want to inspect that companion repo before approving.
4. **`claude-bridge` harness id naming** — there's no precedent for "<vendor>-bridge" harness names; renamable. (The reason I did this initially was to ensure that I wasn't representing that this work was by anthropic -- matters more on the server naming than the in-openclaw extension tho)

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Anthropic-model agents previously had no in-tree harness; the bridge unlocks native tool runs against Claude through the same JSON-RPC pattern codex uses.
- **Real environment tested:** Author's openclaw gateway running on Linux (WSL2 / Ubuntu), Node 22.x. Author's agents  are configured with `claude-bridge` as their harness and are in daily active use against `claude-sonnet-4-6` and `claude-opus-4-7`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. `pnpm install && pnpm build`
  2. `openclaw gateway restart --deep`
  3. Send a Slack DM to one of the claude-bridge agents
  4. Agent responds with native Anthropic tools used; transcript file contains the projected events.
  5. `/claude status` in the same thread prints shared-client liveness.
- **Evidence after fix (fresh capture, 2026-05-28):** This gateway runs the claude-bridge harness as the default runtime for all Anthropic models (`agents.defaults.models.*.agentRuntime.id = "claude-bridge"`), so the harness is exercised continuously across multiple agents. Captured live:
  1. **Bridge liveness** — shared-client lifecycle today: `claude-bridge: spawned ×5 / initialized ×5 / process exited ×5` (restarts and re-initializes without manual intervention).
  2. **A turn completing with visible tool/progress output** — one bridge turn dispatching consecutive tools, each completing, with per-tool provenance timing from the observability plugin: `ToolSearch → after_tool_call 1.9ms`, `exec → 1.7ms`, `Write → 1.9ms`, `Agent → 1.9ms`.
  3. **Transcript / result event stream** — the run persists a projected event stream to `~/.openclaw/agents/<id>/sessions/<uuid>.jsonl`: parentId-chained `message` events covering the user turn, assistant `tool_use` blocks, and `tool_result` blocks.
  4. **`/claude status`** (live, from a bridge-backed agent):
     ```
     Claude app-server status
     Shared client: running
     Command: openclaw-claude-bridge
     Last stderr: [turn-runner] system event {"subtype":"init"} / {"subtype":"status"} / unhandled SDK event {"type":"rate_limit_event"}
     ```
  *Log-hygiene note:* a few `claude-bridge: runAttempt failed` lines appear in the same logs; they are not bridge defects — one class is a context-overflow on a zero-message scheduled run (auto-compaction correctly skipped), the other is transient model-call failover during an MCP reconnect; both recovered on the next turn. Earlier daily-driver context still applies: Tabitha (claude-bridge) has been in active production use for ~2 weeks.
- **Observed result after fix:** Anthropic turns complete normally; tool calls fire; rate-limit messages are user-actionable; resume across restart works via the `.claude-binding.json` sidecar.
- **What was not tested:**
  - Windows/macOS gateway hosts (only Linux/WSL2 verified).
  - Bun runtime (Node 22 only).
  - Anthropic enterprise/Bedrock/Vertex API surfaces (only public Anthropic API).
  - Concurrent multi-account scenarios beyond 3 agents.
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** Author runs a single-host gateway. The server is currently published under the author's `@zeroaltitude` npm scope (fork-preview), so reproducing this requires installing the bridge from that scope until maintainers republish.
- **Before evidence:** N/A — no prior in-tree harness for Anthropic to compare against.

## Tests and validation

- Real world use for over a month on a broad diversity of tasks
- `pnpm build` ✓ (local)
- `pnpm test extensions/claude` ✓ (local — extension unit tests, including event projector, dynamic-tools middleware port, thread fork policy envelope, transcript mirror dedupe)
- `pnpm test:extensions` not run since last rebase — recommend Testbox/Crabbox run before non-draft review.
- No `CHANGELOG.md` edit per contributor rule; maintainer to add on land.

## Risk checklist

- **Did user-visible behavior change?** Yes — new `claude-bridge` harness appears in model picker; new `/claude` slash command surface.
- **Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** Yes — new plugin `configSchema` (see `extensions/claude/openclaw.plugin.json`). Existing agent configs unaffected unless they explicitly opt into `claude-bridge` harness. Doctor contract added (`extensions/claude/doctor-contract-api.ts`).
- **Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** Yes (intentional). The bridge spawns a subprocess that holds the Anthropic API key in-memory and makes network calls to Anthropic. Subprocess lifecycle is owned by the harness, not user shell. The plugin SDK enabler changes (e.g. `command-registration.ts`) are additive and follow existing codex-extension precedent.
- **Highest-risk area:** Server-bridge stdio lifecycle and the few SDK enabler edits in `src/plugins/**`. If the SDK changes accidentally relax a guard for non-claude paths, every harness would inherit the change.
- **How is that risk mitigated?** The SDK changes are narrowly scoped — each follows a codex-extension-shaped precedent. Happy to factor them into a prep PR if reviewers want to land them separately first.

## Current review state

- **Next action:** Maintainer steer on scope: (a) accept as bundled extension as-is, (b) split SDK enablers into a prep PR first, or (c) request packaging as an external plugin.
- **Still waiting on:** Maintainer decision on the above. CI run on this push.
- **Bot/reviewer comments addressed:** None yet — opening draft.

---

🤖 **AI-assisted disclosure:** Developed with the assistance of Claude Code (anthropic.com/claude-code). All design decisions, production deployment, and real-behavior verification are human-driven; AI assistance was used for implementation across multiple sessions. Session transcripts available on request.
