target.title: feat(openrouter): forward OpenClaw session id as session_id
target.body:
Optionally forward the active OpenClaw session id to OpenRouter as the chat-completion session_id body field. OpenRouter uses it as a sticky routing key (better prompt-cache hits) and for observability. Opt-in per the OpenRouter plugin config (forwardSessionId), off by default; the id is clamped to OpenRouter's 256-char cap and never overrides a caller-set value. ## Summary What problem does this PR solve? * OpenRouter accepts a `session_id` to route requests to the same upstream provider, which maximizes prompt-cache hits and improves observability[cite: 1]. * OpenClaw already generates a stable per-session ID, but it was not being forwarded to OpenRouter[cite: 1]. Why does this matter now? * Forwarding the session ID allows OpenRouter to keep a multi-turn OpenClaw session on one upstream provider[cite: 1]. * This improves cache economics and traceability for users[cite: 1]. What is the intended outcome? * Optionally forward the active OpenClaw session ID to OpenRouter as the `session_id` request-body field on chat-completion calls[cite: 1]. * This feature is opt-in, configured per model provider (OpenRouter) via the OpenRouter plugin config, and is off by default[cite: 1]. * The ID is clamped to OpenRouter's 256-character limit[cite: 1]. * If a caller-set value already exists, it is never overridden[cite: 1]. What is intentionally out of scope? * Image, music, video, media-understanding, or speech OpenRouter providers (this is for chat/text inference only)[cite: 1]. * Generic provider session forwarding (this is an OpenRouter-specific field)[cite: 1]. * Changing core SDK context types or the shared transport turn-state hook[cite: 1]. * Sending the `x-session-id` header (the body field is sufficient and takes precedence)[cite: 1]. What does success look like? * When enabled, the `session_id` is injected into the OpenRouter chat-completion body[cite: 1]. * When disabled, there is no change to existing behavior (byte-identical payloads)[cite: 1]. What should reviewers focus on? * The implementation in `extensions/openrouter/stream.ts`, specifically the payload-patch wrapper[cite: 1]. * The addition of the boolean flag in the OpenRouter plugin's `configSchema` (`extensions/openrouter/openclaw.plugin.json`)[cite: 1]. * The edge case handling (flag off, caller-set value, over-long ID)[cite: 1].
Summary guidance This PR description is the contributor's durable explanation of the change. Write it for human maintainers first; ClawSweeper and Barnacle use the same text to understand intent, proof, risk, and current review state. Describe the intent and outcome in 2-5 bullets. Avoid restating the diff; reviewers and bots can read the changed files. If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(): beta blocker - ` and link the matching `Beta blocker: - ` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.
## Linked context Which issue does this close? Closes # Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related? Related # Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?
Linked context guidance Link the issue, PR, discussion, maintainer request, or owner request that explains why this PR should exist. Maintainer context helps reviewers and automation distinguish intended work from drive-by churn.
## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: Forwarding OpenClaw session id to OpenRouter as `session_id`. - Real environment tested: Local macOS environment. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: Enabled `forwardSessionId` and the debug proxy in OpenClaw, then sent a chat request. - Evidence after fix: 1. Intercepted payload captured in data blob `f8acf6e6aa9c226d0a30bdf5`. 2. Extracted `session_id` (`cc8e2503-a840-4fbe-814f-58ff91702d84`) from the proxy blob. 3. OpenRouter dashboard confirming the matching Session ID. 4. The captured terminal output shows the payload sent to OpenRouter correctly includes the generated `session_id` alongside the model and messages. ```text $ git rev-parse --short HEAD && git branch --show-current 913750c2c6 feature/session-forwarding-openrouter $ zcat config/debug-proxy/blobs/f8acf6e6aa9c226d0a30bdf5.bin.gz | grep "{" | jq '.' { "model": "xiaomi/mimo-v2.5-pro", "messages": [ { "role": "system", "content": "You are a personal assistant running inside OpenClaw..." } ], "tool_choice": "auto", "max_completion_tokens": 32000, "session_id": "cc8e2503-a840-4fbe-814f-58ff91702d84" } ``` - Observed result after fix: The session ID was correctly injected into the payload and successfully recognized by OpenRouter. - What was not tested: Non-OpenRouter providers. - Proof limitations or environment constraints: None. **Screenshot of the behaviour on OpenRouter:** **After-fix screenshot:** ## Tests and validation Which commands did you run? * `pnpm test extensions/openrouter`[cite: 1] What regression coverage was added or updated? * Unit tests in the extension for flag on/off, caller-set payload, and over-256 ID[cite: 1]. * Negative testing to ensure non-OpenRouter routes do not get `session_id`[cite: 1]. What failed before this fix, if known? * N/A (Additive feature) If no test was added, why not? * N/A
Testing guidance List focused commands, not every incidental check. CI is useful support, but external PRs still need real behavior proof above when behavior changes.
## Risk checklist Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) * No (Opt-in feature, default off)[cite: 1] Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) * Yes (New opt-in flag added to OpenRouter plugin configSchema)[cite: 1] Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) * No What is the highest-risk area? * The payload-patch wrapper in `extensions/openrouter/stream.ts`[cite: 1]. How is that risk mitigated? * The feature is additive and default-off[cite: 1]. Existing payloads remain unchanged when disabled[cite: 1]. * The wrapper includes checks to never override a caller-set value and clamps the ID to 256 characters[cite: 1].
Risk guidance Use this for author judgment that is not obvious from the diff. ClawSweeper can see touched files, but it cannot know which behavior you think is risky, why the risk is acceptable, or what mitigation reviewers should verify.
## Current review state What is the next action? * Awaiting review[cite: 1]. What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? * Maintainer decisions on opt-in location (plugin `configSchema.forwardSessionId` vs. `models.providers.openrouter.params`)[cite: 1]. * Maintainer decision on flag name[cite: 1]. * Confirmation on scope (chat/text inference only)[cite: 1]. * Confirmation on subagent session behavior[cite: 1]. Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?
Review state guidance Keep this as the durable state for review progress. If useful information appears in comments, fold the current next action or blocker back here so maintainers and ClawSweeper do not need to reconstruct state from comment history.

target.title: fix(memory): use FTS5 snippet() for keyword search instead of truncating chunk text from the beginning
target.body:
## Problem

Keyword search results show the first N characters of the chunk text as the snippet (via ). When a match occurs deep in a chunk (e.g., line 50+ of a large chunk), the snippet only displays the beginning of the chunk — the match is invisible to the user.

Reported in #47730.

## Fix

Use SQLite FTS5's built-in `snippet()` function for keyword search, which extracts the relevant passage around the match with configurable ellipsis markers. This returns context centered on the actual match instead of the first lines of the chunk.

## Changes

- `src/memory/manager-search.ts`: Replace `SELECT ... text` with `SELECT ... snippet(table, 0, '…', '…', '', N) AS snippet` in the FTS query.

## Testing

- Existing tests should pass (the snippet column is just a different source for the same field)
- Manual test: index a file with content on line 50+, search for that content → snippet should show the relevant passage, not lines 1-15

target.title: feat: adaptive resource limits for ARM/low-memory devices
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `detectResourceProfile()` utility (`low`/`standard`/`high`) based on total RAM and CPU architecture, providing a single detection point for constrained-device tuning
- Adapts FFmpeg buffer sizes, image resize grid dimensions, quality compression steps, embedding concurrency, and plugin cache limits on low-profile (ARM/<2GB) devices
- Adds systemd `MemoryHigh`/`MemoryMax` cgroup limits for low-profile daemon installs so the gateway stays within safe bounds on Raspberry Pi
- Lowers LanceDB vector search limits on <2GB devices in the `memory-lancedb` extension (uses `node:os` directly since extensions are standalone packages)
- Adds Raspberry Pi platform documentation (`docs/platforms/raspberry-pi.md`)

### Commits

1. `infra: add platform resource profile detection for ARM/Pi tuning` — foundation module with `detectResourceProfile()` and tests
2. `feat: adaptive resource limits for ARM/low-memory devices` — consumers across media pipeline, memory, plugins, and docs
3. `fix(test): set OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR in bundle plugins tests to prevent timeout` — test environment fix for plugin loader tests
4. `feat: adaptive systemd cgroup limits for low-memory devices` — extends systemd unit generation with memory cgroup limits

### Files changed

- `src/infra/platform-profile.ts` (new) — profile detection logic
- `src/infra/platform-profile.test.ts` (new) — profile detection tests
- `src/media/ffmpeg-limits.ts` — adaptive buffer sizes
- `src/media/ffmpeg-exec.ts` — use adaptive limits
- `src/media/image-ops.ts` — adaptive resize grids and quality steps
- `src/memory/manager-embedding-ops.ts` — adaptive embedding concurrency
- `src/plugins/loader.ts` — adaptive plugin cache size
- `src/plugins/loader.test.ts` — updated plugin loader tests
- `extensions/memory-lancedb/index.ts` — adaptive LanceDB search limits
- `src/daemon/service-types.ts` — memory limit fields in service types
- `src/daemon/systemd-unit.ts` — cgroup limit generation
- `src/daemon/systemd-unit.test.ts` — systemd unit generation tests
- `src/daemon/systemd.ts` — pass profile to unit generator
- `docs/platforms/raspberry-pi.md` — Pi platform docs

## Test plan

- [ ] `pnpm build` — typecheck passes
- [ ] `pnpm test -- src/infra/platform-profile.test.ts` — profile detection tests pass
- [ ] `pnpm test -- src/plugins/loader.test.ts` — plugin loader tests pass
- [ ] `pnpm test -- src/daemon/systemd-unit.test.ts` — systemd unit tests pass
- [ ] `pnpm check` — lint/format clean
- [ ] Manual: verify on a Raspberry Pi 4 (2GB) that gateway starts with reduced memory footprint

target.title: fix(agents): skip tool prep when models disable tools
target.body:
## Summary

- Skip core/plugin tool construction when the active model declares `compat.supportsTools: false`.
- Keep existing raw-model and explicit no-tools behavior, while making the documented no-tools small-model fallback avoid tool-prep overhead too.
- Add planner and runner-level coverage proving forced message delivery cannot re-enable tool construction for tool-disabled models.

Refs #86599.
Related: #88142 covers the same optimization at a later call site; this PR keeps the decision in the construction plan so downstream code sees the correct no-tools state.

## Verification

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed on pushed SHA `e5f59b7a63c`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.cwd-split.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed on pushed SHA `e5f59b7a63c`: 2 routed Vitest shards, 25 tests, 9.16s.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.cwd-split.test.ts` passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.cwd-split.test.ts` passed.
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` passed with exit 0 on pushed SHA `e5f59b7a63c`: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_e3f7d477f364`, run `run_eaaf1556be1f`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, lanes `core`, `coreTests`.
- Branch autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings; overall patch correct 0.85.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: models configured with `compat.supportsTools: false` no longer pay to construct the OpenClaw tool surface before the runner discards it.

Real environment tested: focused local node-wrapper Vitest in a Codex worktree; AWS Crabbox Linux `c7a.8xlarge` changed gate.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.cwd-split.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target linux --idle-timeout 60m --ttl 120m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`.

Evidence after fix: focused planner and runner regressions passed 25 tests on pushed SHA `e5f59b7a63c`; AWS Crabbox `run_eaaf1556be1f` completed `pnpm check:changed` with exit 0 and covered the `core`/`coreTests` lanes.

Observed result after fix: `resolveEmbeddedAttemptToolConstructionPlan` returns `constructTools: false`, `includeCoreTools: false`, and no runtime allowlist when `toolsEnabled: false`; the runner-level regression confirms `createOpenClawCodingTools` is not called when the selected model reports no tool support.

What was not tested: no live Ollama/API small-model agent turn in this PR; this PR removes local tool-prep overhead for toolless model metadata and does not change provider transport or model output handling.

target.title: Android: lower minSdk to 26
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Current minSdk version was too high, preventing users on older Android devices (API 26-30) from installing the app. Additionally, the UI relied on dynamic color features (Material You) available only in newer Android versions, which would cause crashes or rendering issues on older APIs.
- Why it matters: Lowering the minSdk to 26 (Android 8.0) significantly expands our potential user base while maintaining support for essential modern background limits and security features.
- What changed:
  - Updated build.gradle to set minSdk 26.
  - Added theme color fallbacks for devices that do not support Dynamic Color (API < 31).
  - Refactored NodeForegroundService.kt to include NotificationChannel setup and compliant startForeground logic for API 26+.
  - Added a conditional check for DnsResolver to prevent crashes on Android 8.0-10, with an automatic fallback to a direct UDP resolver (port 53).
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes were made to the core business logic or higher-level API features (API 31+); those users will still experience dynamic coloring.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Users on Android 8.0 (Oreo) through Android 11 can now install and run the app.
- On Android 12+, the app continues to use dynamic system colors.
- On Android 8.0-11, the app will use a consistent, predefined brand color palette.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Android 8.0 (API 26) Emulator / Android 14 (API 34) Physical Device
- Runtime/container: Android
- Model/provider: Pixel 2 (Emulator), Pixel 7 (Physical)
- Integration/channel (if any):
- Relevant config (redacted):

### Steps

1. Build and install the app on an Android 8.0 (API 26) device/emulator.
2. Start the Node service and verify that the Foreground Notification appears correctly (validating the NotificationChannel setup).
3. Test network-related features (like gateway discovery) to ensure the DNS fallback works.

### Expected

- The app installs and opens successfully on API 26.

### Actual

- App launches without crash; theme gracefully falls back to static brand colors on API 26.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets [build_output.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26076480/build_output.txt)
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: App launch, main navigation, and settings screen on API 26.
- Edge cases checked: Dark mode toggle on older Android versions (ensure values-night also has fallbacks).
- What you did **not** verify: Did not test on every single API level between 26 and 31 (sampled 29).

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert the minSdk change in build.gradle and roll back OpenClawTheme.kt file.
- Files/config to restore: app/build.gradle, OpenClawTheme.kt.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: UI crashes (Resource Not Found) on older devices if a fallback color is missing.

## Risks and Mitigations

None

target.title: fix(nextcloud-talk): parse rich object file shares from webhook payload
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #29152

When a user shares a file or image in Nextcloud Talk, the bot webhook receives the message but the NC Talk plugin was not parsing the rich object parameters — so the agent never saw the attachment.

NC Talk encodes file shares as rich object messages where `object.content` is a JSON string:
```json
{"message":"{file}","parameters":{"file":{"type":"file","id":"12345","name":"IMG_123.jpg","path":"Talk/IMG_123.jpg","link":"https://cloud.example.com/f/12345","mimetype":"image/jpeg","preview-available":"yes"}}}
```

Previously `payloadToInboundMessage()` passed this raw JSON string as `text`, leaving `mediaUrls` unpopulated.

## Changes

### `monitor.ts`
- Adds `parseRichObjectContent()` helper that detects JSON-encoded rich object payloads
- Extracts file links into `mediaUrls` (https/http only — rejects `file://`, `javascript:`, etc.)
- Resolves `{placeholder}` names for human-readable `displayText` (covers file shares, mentions, deck cards, polls, etc.)
- Uses `replaceAll` for placeholder substitution to handle duplicate occurrences
- Falls back to raw `content` for non-rich-object text — no behaviour change for regular messages

### `types.ts`
- Adds `mediaUrls?: string[]` to `NextcloudTalkInboundMessage`

### `inbound.ts`
- Passes `MediaUrls` into `finalizeInboundContext` (verified: already consumed natively as `string[]`)
- Relaxes the empty-body guard to allow media-only messages through (previously silently dropped)

## Tests

- `monitor.attachment.test.ts` — 7 unit tests: file share, text passthrough, malformed JSON fallback, name fallback, mention resolution, `file://` URL rejection, multi-file
- `monitor.live-integration.test.ts` — 5 integration tests using real HMAC-signed payloads against the webhook server

All 12 tests pass. TypeScript compiles clean (`tsc --noEmit`).

## Before / After

**Before:** Sharing a file in NC Talk → agent receives raw JSON string, never sees the attachment.

**After:** Sharing a file → agent receives `text = "filename.jpg"` and `mediaUrls = ["https://..."]` — can immediately process the image via vision without any manual workaround.

target.title: feat(ui): add generated image actions
target.body:
## Summary

Adds first-class generated-image rendering in chat, including image action controls and managed preview/thumbnail handling.

- Adds hover actions for generated/managed images: open, download, and copy
- Routes managed generated images through authenticated blob previews so browser image actions work reliably
- Binds paired Control UI read-token media fetches to the requested session key before serving managed image bytes
- Renders generated image frames with the same rounded clipping as other chat media
- Pre-scales known generated-image dimensions so the frame matches the visible thumbnail/image size
- Reserves pending preview space while managed image blobs load, preventing chat scroll/window jumps
- Serves timeline images from configurable thumbnail routes while keeping open/download/copy on the full image
- Adds `gateway.controlUi.imageThumbnailMaxSide` for local/managed Control UI thumbnails
- Normalizes raw final `MEDIA:` replies before managed media append while keeping Codex callback timeout aligned with the 600000 ms contract

## Why

Generated images were being displayed without the full media affordances we expect in chat. The implementation also needed stable thumbnail/frame geometry so the new actions attach to the visible image, rounded corners clip the actual image, and the chat viewport does not jump while a managed preview is still loading.

The follow-up fixes make the preview/full-image split explicit: right-click View Image uses the thumbnail preview, while click/Open/Download/Copy use the original full-size image.

## Testing

- `corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`

target.title: #92715: Fix Discord intermediate status reaction emojis missing during tool/skill execution
target.body:
## Summary

Fix Discord intermediate status reaction emojis (🔧, 🔍, 🛠️, etc.) not displaying during tool/skill execution phases. The dispatch callback wrapper's `requiresToolSummaryVisibility` gate was blocking the entire `onToolStart` callback when verbose was off (default config), preventing `statusReactions.setTool()` from being called.

## Root Cause

In `dispatch-from-config.ts`, the `shouldForwardProgressCallback` gate drops callbacks with `requiresToolSummaryVisibility: true` when `shouldSendToolSummaries()` returns false (verbose off by default). Discord's `onToolStart` was registered with `requiresToolSummaryVisibility: true`, causing both the status reaction emoji update **and** the text progress to be blocked in the default configuration.

The fix adds a `bypassToolSummaryVisibility` option to the progress callback wrapper. When set to `true` on `onToolStart`, the callback always fires for status reaction purposes, while the text progress (`pushToolProgress`) — which adds to an inactive draft buffer when streaming is off — is handled separately by the channel adapter.

## Real behavior proof

### behavior

The fix decouples Discord status-reaction lifecycle updates from the verbose/tool-summary delivery gate. `onToolStart` callbacks now always reach the Discord adapter, allowing `statusReactions.setTool()` to update the reaction emoji during tool execution. Text progress summaries remain suppressed unless verbose or streaming-preview settings explicitly allow them.

### environment

- OS: Windows 10 Enterprise LTSC 2019 + WSL (repo), Ubuntu 24.04 (AWS EC2 t3.small, issue reporter)
- Runtime: Node.js 22.x, pnpm 10.x
- Setup: openclaw main branch, vitest test runner, Discord integration via standard plugin

### steps

1. Send a Discord message triggering multi-step tool processing (e.g., web_fetch → exec → exec with filter)
2. Observe the Discord reaction sequence on the user's message
3. Before fix: Only 👀 (initial), ⏳ (processing), and 🧠 (final) appear — no intermediate tool-phase reactions
4. After fix: 👀 → ⏳ → 🔍 (web_fetch) → 🔧 (exec) → 🧠 — intermediate reactions appear during each tool call

### observedResult

**Source-level verification (repro-92715.mjs):**
```
=== Source-level verification of bypassToolSummaryVisibility ===

[PASS] shouldForwardProgressCallback checks bypassToolSummaryVisibility
[PASS] wrapProgressCallback declares bypassToolSummaryVisibility option
[PASS] onToolStart sets bypassToolSummaryVisibility: true

=== Gate logic (shouldForwardProgressCallback) ===

Before fix:
  if (requiresToolSummaryVisibility && !shouldSendToolSummaries() && ...)
    -> return false  // BLOCKS onToolStart when verbose=off

After fix:
  if (!bypassToolSummaryVisibility && requiresToolSummaryVisibility && ...)
    -> bypass allows forwarding  // onToolStart fires regardless

bypassToolSummaryVisibility appears 4x in dispatch-from-config.ts
Expected: 4x (1 in shouldForward type, 1 in gate check, 1 in wrap type, 1 in onToolStart)
[PASS] Count check

=== Other callbacks unchanged ===
[PASS] onPartialReply: bypassToolSummaryVisibility absent (correct)
[PASS] onReasoningStream: bypassToolSummaryVisibility absent (correct)
[PASS] onReasoningEnd: bypassToolSummaryVisibility absent (correct)
[PASS] onAssistantMessageStart: bypassToolSummaryVisibility absent (correct)
[PASS] onItemEvent: bypassToolSummaryVisibility absent (correct)
[PASS] onCommandOutput: bypassToolSummaryVisibility absent (correct)
```

**Test suite (status-reactions):**
```
 RUN  v4.1.7 D:/Projects/openclaw

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  43 passed (43)
```

**Test suite (Discord message-handler.process):**
```
 RUN  v4.1.7 D:/Projects/openclaw

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  104 passed (104)
```

**Test suite (dispatch-from-config + followup-runner):**
```
 RUN  v4.1.7 D:/Projects/openclaw

 Test Files  4 passed (4)
      Tests  106 passed (106)
```

**Production change:**
```typescript
// dispatch-from-config.ts — shouldForwardProgressCallback gate
// Added bypassToolSummaryVisibility option:
if (
  !options?.bypassToolSummaryVisibility &&  // <-- NEW: skip gate when bypass set
  options?.requiresToolSummaryVisibility === true &&
  !shouldSendToolSummaries() &&
  !shouldAllowQuietChannelOwnedProgressCallbacks(options)
) {
  return false;
}

// onToolStart wrapper now includes bypass:
onToolStart: wrapProgressCallback(params.replyOptions?.onToolStart, {
  forwardWhenSourceDeliverySuppressed: true,
  requiresToolSummaryVisibility: true,
  bypassToolSummaryVisibility: true,  // <-- NEW
  waitForDirectBlockReplyDelivery: true,
  ...
}),
```

## Regression Test Plan

target.title: fix(android): widen chat bubbles and render markdown consistently
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #88014 by making the active Android `ChatScreen` bubbles use a readable 90% width instead of the previous 56% assistant / 64% user caps.
- Routes active chat text through the existing `ChatMarkdown` renderer consistently, matching the sibling `ChatMessageViews` path and avoiding the old syntax detector that skipped some Markdown/autolink cases.
- Adds a focused layout regression test to keep the active chat bubble width from shrinking again.

## Linked context

Closes #88014.

## Root Cause

`ChatScreen` had its own chat bubble implementation separate from `ChatMessageViews`. The active screen capped assistant bubbles at `0.56f` and user bubbles at `0.64f`, which leaves a narrow reading column on Pixel-sized screens. It also only used `ChatMarkdown` when a small heuristic detected Markdown characters, while the sibling chat surface already sends all text parts through the shared Markdown renderer.

## Fix

The active `ChatScreen` now uses the same 90% bubble width as the existing chat message view path. Its text renderer now always delegates to `ChatMarkdown`, so headings, lists, code blocks, links/autolinks, tables, and plain text go through one renderer instead of a split Markdown/plain-text path.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Active Android `ChatScreen` chat bubbles are no longer capped to the narrow 56% assistant / 64% user widths, and assistant/user chat text is rendered through `ChatMarkdown` so links and inline code use the shared Markdown styling.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw Android Play debug APK from this PR head installed on a local Android emulator `openclaw88014` (Pixel 8a profile, Android 16 / API 36, arm64). The app was paired to a real local OpenClaw dev gateway running from this checkout on port 19001 via `adb reverse tcp:19001 tcp:19001` and an OpenClaw setup code generated by `pnpm openclaw --dev qr --json --url ws://127.0.0.1:19001 --token proof-token-88014 --no-ascii`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```text
  sdkmanager --install "emulator" "system-images;android-36;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
  avdmanager create avd -n openclaw88014 -k "system-images;android-36;google_apis;arm64-v8a" --device "pixel_8a"
  emulator -avd openclaw88014 -no-window -no-audio -no-boot-anim -gpu swiftshader_indirect
  adb reverse tcp:19001 tcp:19001
  pnpm openclaw --dev config set gateway.auth.mode none
  pnpm openclaw --dev gateway --allow-unconfigured
  ./gradlew :app:installPlayDebug
  pnpm openclaw --dev qr --json --url ws://127.0.0.1:19001 --token proof-token-88014 --no-ascii
  # pasted the generated setup code into the emulator's Gateway Setup screen, paired, opened Chat, sent:
  **wide markdown** https://docs.openclaw.ai `code`
  adb exec-out screencap -p > /tmp/openclaw-88899-real-chat-after-fix-no-keyboard.png
  adb shell uiautomator dump /sdcard/window.xml
  ```
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Screenshot captured from the emulator at `/tmp/openclaw-88899-real-chat-after-fix-no-keyboard.png`. The screenshot shows the active Android `New chat` screen with status `auto`, an assistant bubble spanning nearly the full chat content width, a blue rendered `https://docs.openclaw.ai` link, and an inline `code` chip. Live gateway/UI output from the same run:
  ```text
  2026-06-01T12:12:06.290+08:00 [gateway] device pairing auto-approved device=404d4f8e66a9364c7edfab3b62569569f023b346ee40f4bd31f276f210168d89 role=node
  2026-06-01T12:12:06.670+08:00 [ws] ⇄ res ✓ chat.history 137ms conn=9c7489f3…0b29 id=a24e1259…8b0d
  2026-06-01T12:12:07.400+08:00 [ws] ⇄ res ✓ config.get 867ms conn=9c7489f3…0b29 id=6abdff06…a938
  Emulator UI dump after the response: header `New chat`, connection state `auto`, assistant text `Received: wide markdown https://docs.openclaw.ai code` at bounds [65,385][916,488].
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The active Android chat rendered the assistant response through Markdown styling and used a wide readable bubble instead of a narrow column; the rendered link/code content wrapped inside the bubble without raw Markdown markers or cramped layout.
- What was not tested: A physical Android device was not used; this proof used the Pixel 8a-sized emulator plus a real local OpenClaw gateway.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The emulator screenshot is local to this validation run rather than attached as a GitHub image asset, so the copied live gateway log and UI dump bounds are included here as durable proof text.

## Validation

- `JAVA_HOME=/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21/libexec/openjdk.jdk/Contents/Home ANDROID_HOME=/opt/homebrew/share/android-commandlinetools ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=/opt/homebrew/share/android-commandlinetools PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21/bin:$PATH" ./gradlew :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.ui.chat.ChatScreenLayoutTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.ui.chat.ChatMarkdownTest`
- `JAVA_HOME=/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21/libexec/openjdk.jdk/Contents/Home ANDROID_HOME=/opt/homebrew/share/android-commandlinetools ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=/opt/homebrew/share/android-commandlinetools PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21/bin:$PATH" ./gradlew :app:assemblePlayDebug`
- `git diff --check`

`./gradlew :app:ktlintCheck` was also attempted. It fails on pre-existing spacing violations in unrelated files (`ChatController.kt`, `GatewayDiscovery.kt`, `PermissionRequester.kt`, `SecurePrefs.kt`, `MicCaptureManager.kt`, and `TalkModeManager.kt`); the changed files were not listed in the ktlint report.

## Screenshot Verification

Captured locally from the paired Pixel 8a-sized emulator after sending Markdown through the real local OpenClaw gateway: `/tmp/openclaw-88899-real-chat-after-fix-no-keyboard.png`.

target.title: Telegram: allow native channel commands in explicitly allowed chats
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Telegram `channel_post` updates could carry regular text, but native slash commands such as `/status` were not authorized
correctly in channel relay setups.
- Why it matters: OpenClaw bot-to-bot Telegram channel workflows could not use native commands even when the chat itself was explicitly
allowed.
- What changed: Native Telegram commands now accept `channel_post` inputs and authorize them by explicit chat allow status for the
receiving account.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not redefine sender-scoped `groups[chatId].allowFrom` semantics for `channel_post`,
because Telegram does not expose the originating human sender on that surface.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Integrations
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #49062

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Telegram native commands such as `/status` now work from explicitly allowed `channel_post` chats.
- Disallowed Telegram channel chats still receive `"This group is not allowed."`
- Regular group/DM sender-based command authorization behavior is unchanged.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

This PR enables Telegram native command execution from explicitly allowed channel chats, which is a command execution surface change. The
risk is bounded by existing explicit chat allow checks. Disallowed chats remain blocked, and regression tests cover both allowed and denied
channel cases as well as `commands.allowFrom.telegram` precedence.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

OpenClaw version: 2026.3.13
Operating system: all OS
Install method: npm global
Model: gpt-5.4
Provider / routing chain: chatgpt oauth



### Steps

1. Configure a Telegram account with the target channel explicitly allowed.
2. Send /status as a channel_post in that Telegram channel.
3. Observe native command authorization and reply behavior.

### Expected

- Explicitly allowed channel chats can execute native commands such as /status.

### Actual

- Before this fix, the command was rejected or dropped on channel_post.
- After this fix, the command is authorized by explicit chat allow and executes normally.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
    - /status from an explicitly allowed Telegram channel now succeeds.
    - Disallowed channel chats remain blocked by chat allowlist checks.
- Edge cases checked:
    - commands.allowFrom.telegram does not block explicitly allowed channel_post commands.
    - commands.allowFrom.telegram does not bypass denied channel_post chat allowlists.
- What you did not verify:
    - Sender-scoped groups[chatId].allowFrom semantics for channel_post
    - Live plugin command behavior on Telegram channels
    - Webhook-specific Telegram deployment paths

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for
maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (Yes)
- Config/env changes? (No)
- Migration needed? (No)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly:
    - Revert this PR, or disable Telegram native commands temporarily with channels.telegram.commands.native: false
- Files/config to restore:
    - extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for:
    - Explicitly allowed Telegram channel /status still returns "You are not authorized to use this command."
    - Disallowed channel chats start executing native commands unexpectedly

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write None.

- Risk:
    - channel_post authorization now uses explicit chat allow rather than sender-scoped identity.
    - Mitigation:
        - This is limited to Telegram channel_post, where Telegram does not expose the originating human sender.
        - Explicit chat allow is still required.
        - Regression tests cover both allow and deny behavior.
- Risk:
    - Future contributors may assume sender-scoped groups[chatId].allowFrom also applies to channel_post.
    - Mitigation:
        - Scope is explicitly documented in this PR.
        - A separate follow-up issue will track channel_post sender-scoped allowlist semantics.

target.title: fix(memory-core): don't run the LLM reranker in vsearch/search modes
target.body:
## What

`searchMode: "vsearch"` / `"search"` are documented as vector/lexical-only modes that must not do LLM work — `buildV2Searches`' own doc comment says they exist "so lexical-only or vector-only modes don't trigger unnecessary LLM/embedding work." But `runQmdSearchViaMcporter` builds `callArgs` without `rerank`, and QMD's unified `query` tool **defaults `rerank: true`**. So every `vsearch`/`search` memory search silently runs the LLM reranker anyway. QMD's own tool docs flag it: *"rerank … (default: true). Set to false for faster results on CPU-only machines."*

## Fix

Pass `rerank: false` for `search`/`vsearch` in `runQmdSearchViaMcporter` (full `query` mode keeps reranking). The cold/CLI path (`buildSearchArgs`) is unaffected — it already maps each mode to qmd's dedicated `vsearch`/`search` subcommands, which don't rerank.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: In `searchMode: "vsearch"`/`"search"`, every memory search silently invoked QMD's LLM reranker. `runQmdSearchViaMcporter` omitted `rerank` from the query `callArgs` and QMD's `query` tool defaults `rerank: true`, contradicting those modes' vector/lexical-only contract.
- Real environment tested: Live OpenClaw gateway (memory-core extension, mcporter warm path) → QMD `qmd mcp` running on an Intel UHD 630 iGPU (Gen9.5), host Linux 7.0.6-2-pve. Each case below is a fresh `qmd mcp` process fed exactly the `callArgs` openclaw builds; host kernel watched via `dmesg`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran a `qmd mcp` client that calls the `query` tool with the patched vsearch args `{"searches":[{"type":"vec","query":"how do I handle errors gracefully"}],"limit":3,"rerank":false}`, and compared it against the unpatched args (no `rerank`). Command: `node demo.mjs false` → spawns `qmd mcp`, sends `tools/call query`.
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture / copied live output):
  ```
  AFTER this patch — openclaw sends rerank:false:
    → {"searches":[{"type":"vec","query":"how do I handle errors gracefully"}],"limit":3,"rerank":false}
    ← 3650ms  OK — 3 hits          new host-kernel GPU HANGs during this call: 0

  BEFORE (current behavior — openclaw omits rerank):
    → {"searches":[{"type":"vec","query":"how do I handle errors gracefully"}],"limit":3}
    Reranking error: [Error: vk::Queue::submit: ErrorDeviceLost]
    ← 12303ms  ERROR: vk::Queue::submit: ErrorDeviceLost   new host-kernel GPU HANGs during this call: 3

  host dmesg during the BEFORE call:
    i915 0000:00:02.0: [drm] GPU HANG: ecode 9:1:8ed1fff2, in libuv-worker [275718]
    i915 0000:00:02.0: [drm] Resetting rcs0 for preemption time out
    i915 0000:00:02.0: [drm] libuv-worker[277683] context reset due to GPU hang
  ```
- Observed result after fix: vsearch now sends `rerank:false`, returns 3 hits with zero host-kernel GPU hangs and no reranker invoked; the unpatched path errors with `ErrorDeviceLost` and produces 3 kernel GPU hangs. In my deployment, warm memory recall went from a CPU-fallback spin to ~126 ms on the GPU.
- What was not tested: the QMD v1 tool-name fallback path (`vector_search`/`search`) on real hardware — only the v2 unified `query` tool was exercised live (the new unit test covers the v2 mcporter `callArgs`); and non-iGPU reranker hardware.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the GPU-hang symptom is specific to a constrained iGPU; on capable hardware the same bug manifests as wasted reranker compute rather than a hang. The 3650 ms above is a fresh-process cold model load — warm recall in my deployment is ~126 ms.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): shown inline in the Evidence block above (BEFORE case → `ErrorDeviceLost` + 3 kernel GPU hangs; `dmesg` excerpt included).

## Tests and validation

- Added a unit test: `vsearch` mode sends `rerank: false` via mcporter (and the existing `query`-mode test still asserts it does **not**). `pnpm test:extension memory-core` passes; type-check (`pnpm tsgo:test`) clean.
- The only failing memory-core test, `dreaming-phases.test.ts > suppresses cleanup warnings during request-scoped narrative fallback`, is already red on clean `main` (verified by stashing this change) and is unrelated to this PR.

---

🤖 **AI-assisted** — implemented with an AI coding agent; the real-behavior proof above is from my own live OpenClaw setup, not AI-generated. *Allow edits by maintainers* enabled. No `CHANGELOG.md` change (per CONTRIBUTING).

target.title: fix(agents): trim web tools in lean mode

## Summary

- extend `localModelLean` so it trims `web_search`, `web_fetch`, and `x_search` along with `browser`, `cron`, and `message`
- keep explicit override behavior for exact, grouped, and wildcard web preserves (`group:web`, `web_*`) while suppressing native OpenAI/Codex web-search injection unless the run preserves web tools
- update local-model docs so the experimental lean-mode contract matches the runtime behavior

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `localModelLean` now removes managed web search/fetch/social search tools and prevents provider-native OpenAI/Codex `web_search` injection unless web tools are explicitly preserved.

Real environment tested: linked OpenClaw gwt worktree with symlinked `node_modules`; AWS Crabbox Linux changed-gate and build proof.

target.title: fix(ts): narrow type guard for api_key in oauth-manager
target.body:
## Summary

Fix TypeScript errors in  phase (tsdown/tsgo) that cause the nightly CI to fail.

### Fix 1 — `src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-manager.ts` (line 329)

```diff
-      if (mainCred?.type !== "oauth") {
+      if (mainCred?.type !== "oauth" || mainCred.type === "api_key") {
```

**Rationale:** The original type guard `mainCred?.type !== 'oauth'` does not narrow `mainCred` to `OAuthCredential` because `ApiKeyCredential` is structurally compatible (shares `type`, `provider`, `email`, `displayName`). TypeScript's structural typing means `ApiKeyCredential` satisfies the narrowed shape, so `.expires` is not guaranteed. Adding `|| mainCred.type === 'api_key'` explicitly excludes the confusing structural overlap, allowing proper narrowing to `OAuthCredential | TokenCredential` — both of which have `.expires`.

### Fix 2 — `src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.ts` (lines 264 and 269)

```diff
-    expiry: buildExpiry(rollup.remainingMs, rollup.expiresAt),
+    expiry: buildExpiry(rollup.remainingMs, rollup.expiresAt!),

-      expiry: buildExpiry(prof.remainingMs, prof.expiresAt),
+      expiry: buildExpiry(prof.remainingMs, prof.expiresAt!),
```

**Rationale:** `aggregateOAuthStatus()` returns `{ expiresAt: number | undefined }`. TypeScript does not narrow `expiresAt` to `number` at the call site of `buildExpiry` even though it is provably not undefined there (the function only sets `expiresAt = Math.min(...expirable)` when `expirable.length > 0`; `expirable` is pre-filtered). The `!` assertion is safe because: (1) the caller only invokes this branch when valid timestamps exist, and (2) TypeScript's control flow cannot track this across function boundaries. The assertion documents programmer intent.

---

Root cause of the nightly CI failure: both errors surface during `pnpm build` → `build:plugin-sdk:dts` in the `QA-Lab - All Lanes` scheduled run.

target.title: Fix stale auto-fallback origin model selection
target.body:
## Summary
- Adds a shared stale auto-fallback origin classifier so reply and agent-command selection agree when recorded fallback origin no longer matches the current primary.
- Defers reply-side model/auth cleanup until after read-only slash/inline actions have dispatched and a real run is about to start.
- Prepares agent-command attempts with stale fallback model/auth cleared in memory, then persists durable cleanup only after a successful visible run and only if the store row still matches the stale fallback pin.

## Maintainer feedback addressed
- One prepared selection result now carries reset reason and persistence behavior through the reply turn.
- Stale-origin repair no longer emits the disallowed-allowlist reset event.
- Preserve-state/non-mutating agent-command runs strip fallback auth for the primary attempt without writing durable cleanup.
- Durable cleanup is guarded against concurrent user model switches.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: stale auto-fallback model pins whose recorded fallback origin no longer matches the current primary were being treated as active stored overrides; the fix prepares the primary selection for the next real run and defers durable cleanup until the run boundary.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on branch `codex/92776-prepared-model-selection-boundary`, commit `30bd429052`, running the patched runtime module through Node 24 with `tsx` from the repository dependencies.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module` from the repository root and imported `createModelSelectionState` plus `isStaleAutoFallbackOriginOverride` from the patched source, using a throwaway session entry that matched a stale auto-fallback origin (`anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet-latest` recorded from old `openai/gpt-4.1`, current primary `openai/gpt-4.1-mini`).
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied terminal output from the live Node command:

```json
{
  "proof": "stale-auto-fallback-origin-boundary",
  "staleBefore": true,
  "before": {
    "selected": "anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet-latest",
    "origin": "openai/gpt-4.1",
    "authProfileOverride": "fallback-auth-profile"
  },
  "during": {
    "preparedSelection": "openai/gpt-4.1-mini",
    "resetModelOverride": true,
    "resetModelOverrideReason": "stale-auto-fallback-origin",
    "storeStillPinnedBeforePersist": "anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet-latest",
    "authStillPinnedBeforePersist": "fallback-auth-profile"
  },
  "after": {
    "persisted": true,
    "providerOverride": null,
    "modelOverride": null,
    "modelOverrideSource": null,
    "fallbackOriginProvider": null,
    "fallbackOriginModel": null,
    "authProfileOverride": null,
    "authProfileOverrideSource": null
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the prepared reply selection uses the current primary `openai/gpt-4.1-mini` while the durable session row remains pinned before persistence, then `persistModelOverrideReset()` clears the stale model override, stale fallback origin, and fallback auth fields together.
- What was not tested: no live external provider API call was sent; this proof exercises the local OpenClaw model-selection runtime and session mutation boundary.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the proof uses a redacted throwaway session entry to avoid publishing real auth profile names.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): maintainer feedback on #92790 identified that the old cleanup was scattered before this prepared-selection boundary existed.

## Verification
- node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts
- pnpm tsgo:core
- pnpm tsgo:test:src
- pnpm lint:core
- CI=1 pnpm check:changed -- --base upstream/main --head HEAD
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/auto-fallback-stale-origin.ts src/agents/agent-command.ts src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/model-selection.ts src/auto-reply/reply/model-selection.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-directives.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-directives-apply.ts

Fixes #92776
Supersedes #92790

target.title: fix(agents): project nullable tool schemas for providers
target.body:
## Summary

- project nullable and string-literal tool schemas into provider-compatible shapes before sending runtime tool schemas to providers
- keep unsupported dynamic-schema diagnostics on the raw serialized schema so projection cannot hide a blocker
- preserve explicit `null` clear sentinels during raw argument validation before provider projection
- preserve non-null union branches when dropping nullable variants, instead of narrowing mixed unions to `string`
- refresh only the prompt snapshots affected by the provider-facing dynamic tool schema

## Verification

Reviewed/proved local head: `8aeaadf963a`  
Reviewed/proved base: `db4990d2605`  
Current note: `origin/main` advanced again after the final review; GitHub CI/mergeability should arbitrate the pushed branch against the newest base.

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/llm-core/src/validation.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts --reporter=dot` - passed 3 shards / 118 tests
- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check` - passed, `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 packages/llm-core/src/validation.test.ts packages/llm-core/src/validation.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts` - passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json packages/llm-core/src/validation.test.ts packages/llm-core/src/validation.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts` - passed
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed on the reviewed base
- private reported-name scrub across the spec, changed TS files, and prompt snapshots - clean
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --prompt 'Review the pruned provider/runtime tool schema projection hardening branch after rebasing onto latest main. Focus only on actionable regressions in provider-facing schema projection, raw validation preservation, non-null union preservation, and prompt snapshot correctness. Do not inspect external repositories unless essential.'` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings, `overall: patch is correct (0.76)`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Provider-facing runtime tool schema projection no longer leaks nullable OpenAPI-incompatible shapes for covered runtime schemas, while raw validation still accepts explicit null clear sentinels and mixed non-null unions remain represented as unions.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Codex/gwt worktree on macOS with shared repo `node_modules`, using the repo Vitest wrapper plus snapshot, format, lint, diff, scrub, and autoreview gates.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: The focused Vitest, prompt snapshot, oxfmt, oxlint, diff-check, private-name scrub, and autoreview commands listed above were run after the final rebase.

Evidence after fix: Focused tests passed 3 shards / 118 tests; prompt snapshots were current; oxfmt, oxlint, and diff-check passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: Nullable provider schema branches are projected to provider-compatible schemas, unsupported dynamic keywords are still reported from raw schemas, explicit null clear values validate correctly, and mixed non-null unions are preserved instead of narrowed.

What was not tested: A live provider turn or full `pnpm check:changed` was not run for this narrow PR; GitHub CI is expected to cover the pushed branch against the latest moving `main`.

target.title: fix(feishu): send full streaming card content
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes Feishu streaming card updates so the CardKit content endpoint receives the full visible markdown text on each update.
- Preserves the existing replace path for final closeouts that remove transient streamed status text.
- Updates focused Feishu streaming-card tests to assert full-content PUT payloads and retry behavior.

## Linked context

Closes #88867.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Feishu streaming cards displayed only the newest suffix/last character because the CardKit content update endpoint overwrites card content, while OpenClaw was sending only deltas.
- Real environment tested: contributor checkout, local Feishu streaming-card unit harness.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true corepack pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/streaming-card.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`.
- Evidence after fix: focused test assertions now verify throttled, natural-boundary, and retry updates send `hello small`, `hello!`, `hello world`, and `hello world!` as complete card content.
- Observed result after fix: 1 Vitest shard passed, 17 tests passed.
- What was not tested: live Feishu/Lark tenant streaming card flow.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this contributor environment does not have safe Feishu app credentials or a tenant/channel for live CardKit proof.

## Tests and validation

- `PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true corepack pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/streaming-card.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
- `git diff --check`

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? Yes.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No.
- Highest-risk area: Feishu streaming card rendering cadence/content. Mitigation: the change only affects the content string sent to the existing CardKit update endpoint, and focused tests cover normal updates plus retry after failed/non-OK updates.

## Current review state

Ready for review. Live Feishu proof still needs maintainer-side or credentialed tenant validation.

target.title: Require signed beta desktop distribution
target.body:
## Summary
- Point beta and alpha desktop builds at channel-specific Sparkle appcasts.
- Make `scripts/make_appcast.sh` honor beta/alpha appcast output paths.
- Require signed beta desktop assets plus `appcast-beta.xml` in the beta verifier.
- Add `--desktop-only` and gate beta release publish before npm/plugin dispatch so beta npm cannot get ahead of signed desktop distribution.
- Repair release-branch CI guard failures surfaced after forced base updates:
  - cron tool nullable-clear affordances now stay provider-compatible with plain JSON-schema `type` values and descriptions carrying `null to clear` semantics;
  - the cron tool schema has an explicit guard against `anyOf`, type arrays, `not`, and `const` leaking back into provider-facing tool schema;
  - prompt snapshots were regenerated from the final schema;
  - the durable-final compatibility alias has an explicit `@deprecated` tag.

## Verification
- `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/release-beta-verifier.test.ts test/scripts/package-acceptance-workflow.test.ts` -> 2 files, 42 tests passed.
- `pnpm test src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` -> cron tool 84 tests passed; cron schema 15 tests passed.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` -> prompt snapshots current, 7 files.
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test --pretty false` -> passed.
- `pnpm check:architecture` -> passed.
- `git diff --check` -> passed.
- Full prior-failing shard reproduction: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_SHARD_NAME=agentic-agents-tools OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=2 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm exec node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts` -> 53 files, 860 tests passed.
- Core-fast local reproduction: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_SHARD_NAME=core-unit-fast OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=2 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm exec node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast-fake-timers.config.ts` -> 2 shards passed; unit-fast shard 1053 files / 9993 tests passed, fake-timer shard 10 files / 140 tests passed.
- Earlier exact CI shard reproduction for prior remote flake: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=<tmp> OPENCLAW_VITEST_SHARD_NAME=agentic-control-plane-startup-health-runtime OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=2 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm exec node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts` -> 7 files, 67 tests passed.
- `ruby -e "require 'yaml'; YAML.load_file('.github/workflows/openclaw-release-publish.yml'); puts 'source yaml ok'"` -> `source yaml ok`.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Beta release publish now fails closed before npm/plugin dispatch when the signed desktop beta distribution is not live, so beta npm cannot get ahead of the macOS Sparkle update channel.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw source checkout at commit `050fa4d197b87875d7ac38f7cee17e782fa6e458`, Node/pnpm repo environment, authenticated `gh` against the real `openclaw/openclaw` GitHub release state.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `pnpm release:verify-beta -- 2026.5.31-beta.4 --tag v2026.5.31-beta.4 --repo openclaw/openclaw --desktop-only` from the patched checkout.
- Evidence after fix: terminal output from the real command showed the new gate inspecting live GitHub release assets and live `appcast-beta.xml`:

```text
$ pnpm release:verify-beta -- 2026.5.31-beta.4 --tag v2026.5.31-beta.4 --repo openclaw/openclaw --desktop-only
Desktop beta distribution is incomplete:
  - GitHub release is missing desktop asset OpenClaw-2026.5.31-beta.4.zip.
  - GitHub release is missing desktop asset OpenClaw-2026.5.31-beta.4.dmg.
  - GitHub release is missing desktop asset OpenClaw-2026.5.31-beta.4.dSYM.zip.
  - desktop beta appcast unavailable: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openclaw/openclaw/main/appcast-beta.xml returned HTTP 404.
```

- Observed result after fix: The desktop-only verifier refused to pass because the signed beta desktop assets and `appcast-beta.xml` are absent; this is the intended fail-closed behavior that `OpenClaw Release Publish` now runs before any beta npm/plugin dispatch.
- What was not tested: A successful end-to-end signed macOS promote was not run from this account because `openclaw/releases` still reports READ permission here and the `mac-release` signing/notary/Sparkle secrets are maintainer-custodied.

## Release-infra dependency
The matching release-infra branch is `reidmcgill/releases:codex/signed-beta-appcast`. This account still has READ on `openclaw/releases`, so upstream PR creation/merge and workflow dispatch require an upstream maintainer.

target.title: feat: channel echo / session pinning
target.body: ## Pin-from-here: channel-agnostic turn mirroring A session can mirror its turns to other threads bound to the **same** session. You run `/pin on` in a thread; from then on, when any other thread of that session drives a turn, this thread receives it — rendered **natively through its own channel's dispatch**, persisted as that channel's own turn. ### Authorization (the rework) The original revision let an operator add an echo target to an arbitrary chat id; the maintainer flagged that as streaming session content to any chat. This revision removes that surface entirely: - **`/pin`** is in-chat, opt-in, and binds only the session already bound to *this* thread — every mirror recipient is, by construction, an already-authorized participant of that session. There is no "stream to an arbitrary chat id" path. - Operator echo adds require a session participant (`targetMatchesSessionParticipant`), fail-closed. ### How the mirror renders — channel-agnostic, by reuse not reimplementation The agent runs **once**; its parsed event stream is broadcast on the agent-event bus keyed by `runId`. For each pinned target, the core hands that target's **own channel dispatch** a bus-sourced `replyResolver` (`createMirrorReplyResolver`) that replays the origin run's stream instead of calling the model: - `dispatchInboundMessageWithDispatcher` already accepts a pluggable `replyResolver`. `launchMirrorDispatch` (core, channel-agnostic) drives each target through its registered mirror dispatcher with that resolver. - Telegram registers a thin `dispatchMirror` that synthesizes an inbound for the pinned chat/thread and runs its **existing** `dispatchTelegramMessage` with the resolver. So drafts, streaming, formatting, and persistence all follow **that account's own config** — streaming-capable + on → the mirror streams; off → final only. Nothing is forced; nothing is reimplemented per channel. - **Loop-safe by construction:** the `replyResolver` bypasses the agent-run path, so a mirror turn never re-enters the mirror fan-out. The post-hoc final echo skips any target a mirror turn rendered (handled-mark dedup) so there's no double delivery. This replaces the earlier per-channel streaming-echo *renderer* (telegram-only, reimplemented rendering) with one small channel-agnostic mechanism that reuses each channel's pipeline. **Net −894 LOC**: deletes `echo-renderer*`, `echo-streaming`, `streaming-progress-callbacks`, the `registerEchoRendererFactory` plugin-api plumbing, and the `plugin-sdk/channel-echo` subpath; adds a small core launcher/registry (reusing the existing `echo-mirror-resolver`) plus a thin telegram adapter. ### Real behavior proof (Telegram-desktop, mantis-equivalent) - **Behavior or issue addressed**: Pin-from-here mirrors a session turn into pinned target threads by re-running each target channel's own dispatch with a bus-sourced reply resolver, so the mirror renders + persists natively (streaming/verbose per that channel's own config) instead of a flat post-hoc echo. - **Real environment tested**: A real OpenClaw gateway built from this branch in a Linux crabbox with a desktop; a real Telegram bot + a real Telegram account on Telegram Desktop; a real `claude-cli` backend (`sonnet-4-6`/`opus-4-8`, live Anthropic API) with exec tools; the tdlib user-driver sending real messages. This recreates the Mantis telegram-desktop proof flow locally (same mechanism: telegram-desktop + user-driver + screen capture). - **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Started the gateway from the built branch; in a Telegram thread sent `/new`, `/verbose on`, `/pin on`; then drove the same session from webchat (`chat.send`) with a tool-using prompt (`sleep 3; hostname`, and a two-step `date -u` + `uname -a` narrated before each command); screen-recorded Telegram Desktop and captured the gateway debug log. - **Evidence after fix**: Screenshots on fork branch `proof/pin-from-here-evidence` (linked below) plus gateway-log excerpts — `launchMirrorDispatch` fires for the webchat-origin turn and the telegram mirror runs the full turn (`message processed: channel=telegram messageId=0 … completed`), exactly once (post-hoc echo skipped by the handled-mark). - **Observed result after fix**: The pinned Telegram thread received the webchat-triggered turn's real reply — the actual `hostname` exec output (`crabbox-golden-crayfish-fa445ed1`) — and, under the daily-driver verbose config, the full interleaved durable record (`💬` commentary + `🛠️` tool summaries) rendered natively, with exactly one delivery. - **What was not tested**: A *live* current-head capture of **destination revocation** (the gate is unit-proven and the post-hoc fall-through is closed, but the test-rig group-disable mechanism proved finicky to film). Multi-account routing IS now proven on current head — see below. #### Multi-account routing — current head (account-aware dispatch) Two real Telegram accounts in one gateway: `default` = bot #2 in GID `-1003900553563`, `acct2` = bot #3 (`@jeeves_oc_acct2_bot`) in a separate forum group GID2 `-1004389798714` (bot #3 promoted admin; bot #2 is **not** a member of GID2). The echo target for GID2 is set with `accountId: acct2`, then a **webchat-origin** turn drives the session. Gateway debug log (`.proof/pin-from-here-multiaccount-log.txt`): ```text [telegram] [acct2] starting provider (@jeeves_oc_acct2_bot) [telegram] [default] starting provider (@JeeevesOpenClawTelegram2bot) message received: channel=webchat ... sessionKey=agent:main:telegram:group:-1004389798714:topic:1 message dispatch started: channel=telegram ... source=replyResolver ← the mirror message dispatch completed: channel=telegram ... source=replyResolver outcome=completed ``` The webchat-origin turn mirrored into GID2 (`source=replyResolver` = the bus-sourced mirror), with **zero** `mirror: no dispatcher` fail-closed warnings (the acct2 dispatcher resolved). Because **bot #2 is not in GID2** (`getChatMember` → `chat not found`), a successful mirror delivery there could *only* be the **acct2** account (bot #3) — proving the mirror routes through the target's own account, not the first-registered/default one. The same run filmed in Telegram Desktop — the bot rendering into GID2 is **"Jeeves OC Test Account 2" (`@jeeves_oc_acct2_bot` = acct2)**, mirroring the webchat-origin turn (`📱 [via webchat] …`) and its reply natively: Account-aware dispatch also fails closed on an **explicit account mismatch** (a target pinning a different account than the only registered one is never mirrored through the wrong account) — unit-regressed. Recreation of the Mantis telegram-desktop flow on a local patched crabbox: a real gateway built from this branch (current `openclaw/openclaw` main + this work), Telegram Desktop + the user-driver + screen capture, driven by a **real `claude-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6` backend with an exec tool call** (not a mock). A Telegram thread runs `/pin on`; webchat then drives the same session with a tool-using prompt (`sleep 3; hostname` — output not derivable from context). The pinned thread renders the **webchat-origin turn's reply natively** — including the real tool result — through telegram's own dispatch: 1. `/verbose on` → "⚙️ Verbose logging enabled." 2. `/pin on` → **"📌 Pinned. This thread will mirror turns from other threads of this session."** 3. `📱 [via webchat] Use your exec (bash) tool to run … sleep 3; hostname …` 4. The pinned thread receives the real reply: **"The hostname is `crabbox-golden-crayfish-fa445ed1`."** — the actual box hostname, i.e. a real `hostname` exec run by the webchat-triggered turn, rendered natively in the pinned Telegram thread via the channel-agnostic mirror dispatch. Mechanism confirmed in the gateway log for the filmed run: `launchMirrorDispatch` fires for the webchat-origin turn (origin = webchat, telegram target not self-excluded, dispatcher found); the telegram `dispatchMirror` builds the synthetic inbound (`context=BUILT`) and runs `dispatchTelegramMessage` for the full turn (`message processed: channel=telegram messageId=0 … completed`); exactly one reply lands (post-hoc assistant echo skipped by the handled-mark, no duplicate). #### Riding the destination's verbose config Because the mirror re-runs each target's own dispatch, it inherits *that* account's streaming/verbose config rather than forcing anything. With the daily driver's setup (`verboseDefault: on`, `streaming.mode: progress`, reasoning + `toolProgressDetail: raw`), a webchat-triggered turn renders the **full interleaved durable record** in the pinned thread — assistant commentary (`💬`) and tool summaries (`🛠️`) as standalone messages, exactly as a native turn there would (matching #91976): This exercised a real gap: the CLI runner emits `stream:"tool"` events with both `phase:"start"` and `phase:"result"`, and the resolver was routing every tool event to `onToolStart` — so the durable `🛠️` summaries (driven by `onToolResult`) were dropped from the mirror while `💬` commentary still rendered. Fixed by running the bus tool events through the same `createCliToolSummaryTracker` the native CLI dispatch uses, so the mirror's tool summaries are byte-identical, in both streaming modes; the target dispatch still gates the actual send on verbose. ### Public SDK contract The mirror-dispatcher seam (`registerChannelMirrorDispatcher` / `unregisterChannelMirrorDispatcher` / `MirrorDispatcher`) is now **documented** as a public `channel-outbound` contract in `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md` (new *Channel mirror dispatcher* section): per-account registration, the last-wins reload lifecycle, and the account-match + destination-revocation behavior a channel plugin must honor (the native dispatcher is the sole delivery authority, so it must run the channel's own admission/revocation path). The barrel export points at that section. It stays on its neutral SDK subpath per the repo's internal-module convention rather than being reached into as an internal — final acceptance of the public surface is the maintainers' call. ### Tests + CI - `tsgo:core`: clean. Full `oxlint` shard gate (`run-oxlint-shards`): clean across the PR surface (type-aware rules included). - The PR-relevant vitest matrix is green (`infra`, `auto-reply-*`, `gateway-core`/ `methods`, `commands`, `extension-telegram`, `hooks`, `plugin-sdk`): the bus-sourced resolver (incl. the tool-summary replay), handled-mark dedup, and `/pin` fan-out are covered. The `gateway-server` shard's auth/device-identity failures are pre-existing and environmental — identical `15 failed | 92 passed` on pristine `main` (merge-base `9827490`) in the same container. ### AI assistance Built with AI assistance (Claude). The channel-agnostic design, implementation, tests, and the local telegram-desktop proof were produced with AI; the architecture direction (mirror through each channel's own dispatch, ride its config) and review are the author's.

target.title: fix: cap session context overrides by model window
target.body:
## Summary

- Cap configured session context overrides by the selected model's known native context window.
- Keep context metadata provider-aware across model switches, status resolution, persistence, static catalogs, and gateway config reloads.
- Refresh bundled provider metadata without making provider failures fatal or changing official OpenAI/Anthropic tool schemas.
- Preserve credit for @xdanger's source PR #39859 and report #39857.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/command/session-store.test.ts src/agents/context.lookup.test.ts src/agents/context.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/model-selection.test.ts src/auto-reply/status.test.ts src/commands/status.scan-overview.test.ts src/commands/status.scan.config-shared.test.ts src/commands/status.summary.runtime.test.ts src/commands/status.summary.test.ts src/commands/status.test.ts src/config/io.best-effort.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts` (441 assertions)
- `pnpm tsgo`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs $(git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD -- '*.ts')`
- AWS Crabbox changed gate and build: run `run_27f365e25fd2`, lease `cbx_66c09737cd1a` (`golden-crayfish`), exit 0

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Oversized context overrides and stale model metadata could survive model switches/reloads and make persisted or displayed session context exceed the selected model's known window.

Real environment tested: macOS with official OpenAI Responses and Anthropic Messages APIs; isolated local OpenClaw agent/session/status flow; AWS Linux Crabbox/Testbox.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Forced one official tool call plus follow-up text with OpenAI `gpt-5.5` and Anthropic `claude-sonnet-4-6`; ran `OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 node scripts/test-live.mjs --no-quiet src/agents/models.profiles.live.test.ts`; ran an isolated OpenClaw turn with a 64,000-token configured ceiling and inspected persisted session metadata plus status; ran the AWS changed gate and build listed above.

Evidence after fix: Both official-provider forced tool calls returned the requested `noop` call and follow-up text; all 37 live profile tests passed for both models, including prompt, file-read, and image probes; persisted and status context both resolved to 64,000; AWS changed gate and build completed successfully.

Observed result after fix: Effective context never exceeds the selected model's known window, model switches carry current metadata, and official OpenAI/Anthropic tool calling remains functional.

What was not tested: Live APIs for other providers and native cross-OS application flows. Their metadata paths are covered by focused unit/catalog tests, TypeScript checks, lint, and the Linux remote build.

## Dependency proof

- Anthropic registration and fixed-window handling checked in `extensions/anthropic/register.runtime.ts` and `extensions/anthropic-vertex/provider-catalog.ts`.
- OpenAI Codex model-list context-window behavior checked directly in sibling Codex source at `codex-rs/codex-api/src/endpoint/models.rs` on `b89ce9a2bc`.

Fixes #39857.
Related source PR: #39859.

Co-authored-by: Kros Dai <7087+xdanger@users.noreply.github.com>
